vocabularies.definition: A building where you can read or borrow books.
vocabularies.example:
We read books in the library.
You must be quiet in the library.
I go to the library every Thursday.
quizzes: You can find many interesting books to read in the school __________.
Please remember to be very quiet when you are inside the __________.
I want to borrow a story book from the city __________ today.

vocabularies.definition: A book that explains the meanings of words.
vocabularies.example:
I use a dictionary to learn new words.
Can I borrow your dictionary?
This dictionary is very heavy.
quizzes: If you don't know a word, you can look it up in your __________.
My English __________ is very thick and has many new words in it.
Can I borrow your __________ to check the meaning of this word, please?

vocabularies.definition: A drawing that shows where places like cities, rivers, and mountains are.
vocabularies.example:
We look at the map in geography class.
Can you find Vietnam on the map?
There is a big map on the wall.
quizzes: The teacher showed us a large __________ of the world on the wall.
Can you find where we live on this big __________ of our country?
We use a __________ to find the way when we go to a new city.

grammars.description: Diễn tả sự bắt buộc hoặc cần thiết.
grammars.example:
I have to wear a uniform to school.
We had to do a lot of homework yesterday.

vocabularies.definition: Hair that grows in tight coils or loops.
vocabularies.example:
My sister has long curly hair.
Is your hair straight or curly?
She looks beautiful with curly hair.
quizzes: My sister has beautiful __________ that looks like little springs.
Does your mom have straight hair or __________?
The little girl with __________ is playing with her favorite doll.

vocabularies.definition: The hair that grows on a man's chin and cheeks.
vocabularies.example:
My grandfather has a white beard.
He is growing a beard.
Does your dad have a beard?
quizzes: Santa Claus has a long white __________ and a big red hat.
My dad started growing a __________ because he wants to look like a pirate.
Some men have hair on their chin, which is called a __________.

vocabularies.definition: Having a lot of physical power; not weak.
vocabularies.example:
He is very strong, he can lift the table.
Eat your vegetables to stay strong.
An elephant is a very strong animal.
quizzes: The heavy table is easy for him to lift because he is very __________.
If you want to have a __________ body, you should eat your vegetables.
A big elephant is a very __________ animal that can pull huge trees.

grammars.description: So sánh hơn giữa hai người hoặc vật.
grammars.example:
My brother is taller than me.
This book is better than that one.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of moving on shoes with small wheels.
vocabularies.example:
I go roller-skating in the park.
Do you want to go roller-skating with me?
It is fun to go roller-skating.
quizzes: I love to __________ in the park on weekends because it makes me feel like I am flying.
You should wear a helmet and knee pads when you __________ to stay safe.
My friends and I __________ on the smooth path near the beautiful lake.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of using a camera to make pictures.
vocabularies.example:
I love taking photos of flowers.
He takes photos with his new camera.
Smile! I want to take a photo.
quizzes: I want to __________ of my cute little puppy with my new digital camera.
Can you __________ of us standing in front of the big school building, please?
Smile and look at the camera! I am going to __________ of everyone now.

vocabularies.definition: To travel on water in a boat with a sail.
vocabularies.example:
We can sail on the lake.
He wants to sail around the world.
Can you sail a boat?
quizzes: We can __________ across the blue lake on a small boat with a big white sail.
He wants to learn how to __________ a boat so he can travel around the world.
The white boat with the red __________ looked very beautiful on the water today.

grammars.description: Thì hiện tại tiếp diễn dùng cho kế hoạch tương lai.
grammars.example:
I'm going roller-skating tomorrow.
We're taking photos at the party tonight.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you go to watch movies on a big screen.
vocabularies.example:
We saw a funny movie at the cinema.
Where is the nearest cinema?
Let's go to the cinema this weekend.
quizzes: Would you like to go to the __________ to watch the new superhero movie tonight?
We bought some sweet popcorn and sat in the large, dark room at the __________.
The screen at the __________ is much bigger than the television we have at home.

vocabularies.definition: A large shop that sells food and things for the house.
vocabularies.example:
I help my mom at the supermarket.
The supermarket is near my house.
We buy food at the supermarket.
quizzes: My grandmother goes to the __________ every Saturday morning to buy fresh bread and milk.
We need to get some eggs and juice, so let's go to the __________ near our house.
There are so many different kinds of cereal and snacks on the shelves at the big __________.

vocabularies.definition: A place where sick or injured people go to be treated by doctors.
vocabularies.example:
My aunt works as a nurse in a hospital.
He is in the hospital because he is ill.
The hospital is a big building.
quizzes: The kind nurse helped me when I had to go to the __________ because I hurt my arm.
A __________ is a very large building where doctors work and help people feel better.
My aunt is a doctor, and she works in the city __________ near the park every day.

grammars.description: Giới từ chỉ nơi chốn (opposite, near, above, below).
grammars.example:
The cinema is opposite the park.
The map is above the dictionary.

vocabularies.definition: When the sky is full of clouds and you cannot see the sun clearly.
vocabularies.example:
It is a cloudy day today.
I can't see the sun because it's cloudy.
Cloudy weather is cool.
quizzes: The sky is very grey and __________ today, so I think it might start to rain soon.
When it is __________ outside, we cannot see the bright yellow sun in the sky.
I don't like __________ days because everything looks a bit dark and sad to me.

vocabularies.definition: When there is bright light from the sun and not many clouds.
vocabularies.example:
It's always sunny in the summer.
Put on your hat, it's very sunny.
I like sunny weather.
quizzes: Let's go to the park and play outside because it is a beautiful and __________ day.
You should wear your hat and sunglasses today because the weather is very __________.
I love __________ weather because I can swim in the cool pool with my best friends.

vocabularies.definition: When there is a lot of strong wind or air moving.
vocabularies.example:
The trees are moving because it's windy.
It's a perfect day to fly a kite, it's windy.
My hair is messy because it's windy.
quizzes: It is very __________ today, so it is the perfect time for us to go and fly our colorful kite.
The trees are moving a lot and the leaves are flying away because it is so __________.
My hair is very messy now because the __________ weather blew it all over my face.

grammars.description: Mệnh đề với 'When' để nói về các tình huống.
grammars.example:
When it's sunny, we go to the beach.
I wear a coat when it's cold.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal like a mouse with wings that flies at night.
vocabularies.example:
Bats fly at night.
Bats live in caves.
A bat is a small animal.
quizzes: The small black __________ flies around the trees at night and sleeps during the day.
__________ like to live in dark caves and they hang upside down when they sleep.
A __________ looks like a little mouse but it has wings and can fly very fast.

vocabularies.definition: A large, strong wild animal with thick fur and sharp claws.
vocabularies.example:
The bear is brown and big.
Bears like eating honey.
I saw a bear in the forest.
quizzes: The big brown __________ is sleeping in the cave because it is very cold outside.
A polar __________ lives in the snow and has thick white fur to keep it warm.
Don't wake up the __________! It is very big and strong, and it might be hungry.

vocabularies.definition: A very intelligent sea animal that looks like a large fish.
vocabularies.example:
Dolphins are very clever.
We saw dolphins in the sea.
Dolphins like to jump.
quizzes: The clever __________ jumped out of the blue sea and made a big splash.
__________ are very friendly animals that love to swim and play with humans.
Did you see that __________? It has a very smooth body and a big smile.

vocabularies.definition: An Australian animal that jumps on its strong back legs.
vocabularies.example:
A kangaroo can jump high.
Kangaroos live in Australia.
The baby kangaroo is in its mother's pocket.
quizzes: A __________ has very strong back legs and can jump very high over the grass.
In Australia, you can see a mother __________ carrying her baby in her pocket.
The __________ jumped away very quickly when it saw the people coming near it.

vocabularies.definition: A large wild cat with a mane, known as the king of the jungle.
vocabularies.example:
The lion is the king of the jungle.
Lions are very strong.
I saw a lion at the zoo.
quizzes: The __________ is often called the king of the jungle because it is so brave.
A male __________ has a very large and beautiful mane of hair around its neck.
Listen to the __________! It is making a very loud roar to show everyone its power.

vocabularies.definition: A large black and white animal like a bear that lives in China.
vocabularies.example:
Pandas are black and white.
Pandas like eating bamboo.
They are very cute pandas.
quizzes: The cute __________ is eating green bamboo leaves in the forest in China.
A __________ is a large animal that has thick black and white fur all over.
I saw a funny __________ at the zoo climbing up a tree and then falling down.

vocabularies.definition: A black and white bird that lives in the Antarctic and cannot fly.
vocabularies.example:
Penguins like cold weather.
Penguins can swim but can't fly.
I like watching penguins walk.
quizzes: A __________ is a black and white bird that lives in very cold places but cannot fly.
The __________ is a very good swimmer and likes to jump into the icy water for fish.
I love watching a __________ walk because it waddles from side to side on its short legs.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with long ears and a short tail that lives in holes.
vocabularies.example:
The rabbit has long ears.
Rabbits like eating carrots.
My rabbit is very soft.
quizzes: The __________ has very long ears and likes to eat crunchy orange carrots.
My white __________ is very soft and it lives in a cozy hutch in our garden.
The little __________ hopped away very fast when it heard a loud noise in the grass.

vocabularies.definition: A large sea fish with very sharp teeth that sometimes eats other fish.
vocabularies.example:
Sharks live in the ocean.
Be careful, there's a shark!
Sharks have sharp teeth.
quizzes: Be careful when you swim in the deep ocean because there might be a big __________.
A __________ has many rows of very sharp teeth and is a very fast swimmer.
The grey __________ moved quietly through the water looking for some small fish to eat.

vocabularies.definition: A very large animal that lives in the sea and looks like a fish.
vocabularies.example:
A whale is a very big animal.
Whales live in the sea.
We saw a whale from the boat.
quizzes: The __________ is the largest animal in the world and lives in the deep blue sea.
A __________ can breathe air through a hole on its head when it comes to the surface.
We sat on the boat and watched the giant __________ swim gracefully through the water.

vocabularies.definition: A small insect with two wings that is often found around food.
vocabularies.example:
There is a fly in my soup!
Birds can fly high.
Don't let the fly touch the food.
quizzes: There is a small black __________ buzzing around the kitchen and sitting on the food.
A __________ has two wings and can move very quickly between different places.
Try to catch that __________! It is flying around my head and making a lot of noise.

vocabularies.definition: A young or baby cat.
vocabularies.example:
The kitten is very small.
My cat has three kittens.
I love playing with my kitten.
quizzes: Our cat had four babies, and each tiny __________ is very soft and cute.
The little grey __________ likes to play with a ball of string on the living room floor.
Don't wake up the sleeping __________; it is very tired after playing all morning.

vocabularies.definition: A young or baby dog.
vocabularies.example:
I want a puppy for my birthday.
The puppy is barking.
He is a very cute puppy.
quizzes: I really want a tiny and very cute __________ for my birthday to be my new best friend.
The little __________ is barking at the big ball and jumping up and down happily.
A __________ is a very young dog that needs a lot of love and care from its owners.

vocabularies.definition: A brightly colored bird that can sometimes copy what people say.
vocabularies.example:
The parrot can talk.
Parrots have colorful feathers.
He has a pet parrot.
quizzes: The colorful blue and yellow __________ can copy the funny things that people say.
My pet __________ has very bright feathers and likes to sit on my shoulder all day.
A __________ is an intelligent bird that lives in the tropical jungle with many other birds.

vocabularies.definition: A large animal with a long neck and one or two humps on its back.
vocabularies.example:
Camels live in the desert.
A camel has a hump.
We rode a camel on vacation.
quizzes: A __________ can walk for a very long time in the hot desert without drinking any water.
The large brown __________ has two big humps on its back where it stores some fat.
We sat between the humps and rode the __________ across the dry and sandy hills.

vocabularies.definition: An animal like a small horse with long ears.
vocabularies.example:
The donkey is carrying a heavy bag.
Donkeys look like small horses.
I saw a donkey on the farm.
quizzes: The hardworking __________ is carrying two heavy bags of grain on its back to the village.
A __________ looks a bit like a small horse, but it has much longer ears and a different tail.
We heard the loud 'hee-haw' sound of the __________ coming from the farm down the road.

vocabularies.definition: An animal with horns that lives on farms or mountains.
vocabularies.example:
The goat is eating grass.
Goats give us milk.
My uncle has many goats.
quizzes: The white __________ climbed up the rocky hill to eat some fresh green grass at the top.
Many people drink the healthy milk from a __________ or use it to make delicious cheese.
A __________ has two horns on its head and a little beard under its chin.

vocabularies.definition: A small reptile with a long body, four legs, and a long tail.
vocabularies.example:
The lizard is on the wall.
Some lizards can change color.
I'm not afraid of lizards.
quizzes: The small green __________ sat very still on the big rock to enjoy the warm sunshine.
If a __________ loses its long tail, it can often grow a new one back after some time.
I saw a tiny __________ scurry across the garden path and hide under a large leaf.

vocabularies.definition: A large white bird with a long neck that lives on water.
vocabularies.example:
The swan is white and beautiful.
Swans are swimming on the lake.
An ugly duckling became a swan.
quizzes: The graceful white __________ is swimming slowly across the quiet lake with its babies.
A __________ has a very long and elegant neck and beautiful white feathers all over.
The 'Ugly Duckling' in the famous story eventually grew up to be a very beautiful __________.

vocabularies.definition: A large wild cat with orange and black stripes.
vocabularies.example:
Tigers have orange and black stripes.
A tiger is a wild cat.
The tiger is sleeping.
quizzes: A __________ is a very large and strong wild cat that has orange and black stripes.
The hungry __________ moved very quietly through the tall grass to stay hidden from others.
I saw a very big __________ at the zoo; it was sleeping in the shade of a large tree.

vocabularies.definition: A wild animal from Africa that looks like a horse with black and white stripes.
vocabularies.example:
Zebras have black and white stripes.
I saw zebras in the safari park.
A zebra looks like a horse.
quizzes: A __________ looks like a wild horse but it has many black and white stripes all over its body.
Each __________ has a different pattern of stripes, just like people have different fingerprints.
A large group of __________ was running across the grassy plains in the hot sun in Africa.

vocabularies.definition: A large, heavy animal that lives in rivers in Africa.
vocabularies.example:
The hippo is in the water.
Hippos are very big and heavy.
A hippo can stay underwater for a long time.
quizzes: The large and heavy __________ likes to stay in the cool water of the river to stay cool.
A __________ can open its very big mouth very wide to show its large teeth to everyone.
The __________ is a huge animal that lives in Africa and spends a lot of time underwater.

vocabularies.definition: A very tall African animal with a long neck and spots.
vocabularies.example:
A giraffe has a very long neck.
Giraffes eat leaves from tall trees.
I saw a giraffe at the zoo.
quizzes: A __________ has a very long neck so it can reach the greenest leaves at the top of tall trees.
The __________ is the tallest animal in the world and has beautiful brown spots on its skin.
I was surprised to see how tall the __________ was when I visited the city zoo last month.

vocabularies.definition: A very large grey animal with a long trunk and big ears.
vocabularies.example:
An elephant has a long trunk.
Elephants are the biggest land animals.
The elephant is very strong.
quizzes: An __________ is a very big grey animal that has a long trunk and two very large ears.
The __________ used its long trunk to pick up some water and spray it all over its back.
Because they are so big and heavy, __________ are the strongest animals on the dry land.

vocabularies.definition: An animal with a long tail that lives in trees and looks like a human.
vocabularies.example:
The monkey is climbing the tree.
Monkeys like eating bananas.
That's a funny monkey!
quizzes: The funny __________ is swinging from branch to branch using its long and strong tail.
A __________ looks a bit like a human and loves to eat sweet yellow bananas in the trees.
We watched the clever __________ play with each other in the forest during our school trip.

grammars.description: Diễn tả khả năng.
grammars.example:
A bird can fly, but a penguin can't.
Can you see the dolphin?

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body from your shoulders down to your waist.
vocabularies.example:
My back hurts.
Carry your bag on your back.
Lie on your back.
quizzes: I have to carry my heavy school bag on my __________ every day when I go to school.
My __________ hurts a little bit because I sat in the chair for too long today.
You should lie down on your __________ and look at the stars in the night sky.

vocabularies.definition: The part of the body that joins the head to the shoulders.
vocabularies.example:
I'm wearing a scarf around my neck.
A giraffe has a long neck.
Wash your neck.
quizzes: The tall giraffe has a very long __________ that helps it reach the leaves on trees.
I like to wear a warm wool scarf around my __________ when it is cold in winter.
Please remember to wash your __________ and behind your ears when you take a bath.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body where your arm joins your neck.
vocabularies.example:
He has broad shoulders.
I have a bag on my shoulder.
Touch your shoulders.
quizzes: He carried his little sister on his __________ so she could see the parade better.
I have a small blue bag on my left __________ to keep all my favorite toys in.
Can you touch your __________ with your hands? It's a good way to stay flexible.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body where food goes after you eat it.
vocabularies.example:
I have a stomach ache.
My stomach is full.
Don't eat too much, your stomach will hurt.
quizzes: Don't eat too many sweet candies, or you might get a painful __________ ache.
My __________ feels very full after eating that big bowl of delicious noodles.
The boy rubbed his __________ because he was very hungry and wanted some food.

vocabularies.definition: One of the hard white objects in your mouth used for biting.
vocabularies.example:
I have a loose tooth.
Brush every tooth.
I need to see the dentist for my tooth.
quizzes: I have a loose __________ at the front of my mouth, and it might fall out soon!
The dentist looked at my __________ to make sure it was clean and very healthy.
You should brush every single __________ in your mouth to keep them all strong.

vocabularies.definition: More than one tooth.
vocabularies.example:
Clean your teeth twice a day.
She has white teeth.
How many teeth do you have?
quizzes: It is important to brush your __________ twice a day to keep your smile bright.
Elephants have very long white __________ called tusks that are very strong.
How many __________ do you have? Let's count them in the bathroom mirror now.

vocabularies.definition: A continuous pain that is not very strong.
vocabularies.example:
I have a headache.
I have a toothache.
My legs ache after the run.
quizzes: I have a terrible head __________ today, so I need to lie down in a quiet room.
My legs __________ a lot after running in the school race for a long time today.
If your ears __________ when you swim, you should tell your mom or dad about it.

vocabularies.definition: To push air out of your throat suddenly with a short, loud noise.
vocabularies.example:
He has a bad cough.
Cover your mouth when you cough.
I've had a cough for two days.
quizzes: Please remember to cover your mouth with your hand when you have a loud __________.
He has a bad __________ and a sore throat, so he is staying at home today.
I have had a tiny __________ for two days, so I am drinking lots of warm water.

vocabularies.definition: A continuous pain in your head.
vocabularies.example:
I have a terrible headache.
Do you have a headache?
Take some medicine for your headache.
quizzes: I have a very bad __________ today, so I don't want to listen to any loud music.
Does your __________ feel better now after you took some rest in the dark room?
Taking some cool water and resting can often help make a __________ go away.

vocabularies.definition: A measurement of how hot your body is.
vocabularies.example:
He has a high temperature.
The doctor checked my temperature.
The temperature is 38 degrees.
quizzes: He feels very hot, so the doctor checked his __________ with a thermometer.
If you have a high __________, you should stay in bed and get some good sleep.
My __________ is 38 degrees today, so I think I am a little bit sick now.

vocabularies.definition: Something that you drink or swallow to help you feel better when you are sick.
vocabularies.example:
I have to take my medicine.
This medicine tastes sweet.
Where is your medicine?
quizzes: The sweet pink __________ helped me feel much better after I took it yesterday.
Don't forget to take your __________ after you finish eating your breakfast today.
Where is the __________? I need to take it to help my bad cough go away now.

vocabularies.definition: A place where sick or injured people go to be treated by doctors.
vocabularies.example:
He is in the hospital.
The hospital is very big.
Go to the hospital if you feel very sick.
quizzes: The large city __________ is where people go to see doctors when they are very ill.
My mother works as a nurse in the __________ and helps many sick children every day.
The __________ is a very big and quiet building with many beds for all the patients.

vocabularies.definition: A person who cares for sick or injured people in a hospital.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse is very kind.
My mother is a nurse.
A nurse works in a hospital.
quizzes: The kind __________ gave me a colorful sticker after she checked my temperature.
A __________ works with doctors in the hospital to take care of people who are sick.
My aunt is a very helpful __________ who knows how to help people feel better quickly.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in the inside of your ear.
vocabularies.example:
I have an earache.
It hurts to have an earache.
Does your earache feel better?
quizzes: If your ears start to hurt after you go swimming, you might have an __________.
I have a painful __________ today, so I don't want to listen to any loud music now.
The doctor checked inside my ear to see why I had such a bad __________ this morning.

vocabularies.definition: To produce tears from your eyes because you are sad or hurt.
vocabularies.example:
Don't cry, everything is okay.
The baby is crying.
Why are you crying?
quizzes: Please don't __________; I will help you fix your broken toy and make you feel better.
The little baby started to __________ because she was very hungry and wanted some milk.
Sometimes we __________ when we are very sad or when we hurt our knees while playing.

vocabularies.definition: To speak very loudly because you are angry or want to be heard.
vocabularies.example:
Don't shout in the library!
He shouted my name.
She shouted for help.
quizzes: Please don't __________ in the school library because other students are trying to study.
He had to __________ my name very loudly because the music at the party was so noisy.
If you need some help, you can __________ for me and I will come to help you quickly.

vocabularies.definition: To make your mouth wider to show that you are happy.
vocabularies.example:
Smile for the camera!
She has a beautiful smile.
He smiled at me.
quizzes: Look at the camera and give me a big __________ so I can take a beautiful photo of you!
She has a very happy __________ that always makes everyone else feel very happy too.
He __________ at me when he saw me in the school playground this morning before class.

vocabularies.definition: Having little body fat; not fat.
vocabularies.example:
He is very thin.
This book is very thin.
He has thin arms.
quizzes: The little bird has very __________ legs that look like small sticks in the green grass.
This story book is very __________; I can finish reading the whole thing in ten minutes.
He is quite __________ because he loves to run and play sports every single afternoon.

vocabularies.definition: Having a lot of body fat; not thin.
vocabularies.example:
The cat is very fat.
Don't eat too much fat.
He is a fat panda.
quizzes: The big ginger cat is very __________ because it loves to eat a lot of fish and sleep.
Don't eat too many sweet cakes and candies, or you might become a little bit __________.
A __________ panda looks like a big, soft, and very cuddly black and white teddy bear.

vocabularies.definition: To use a telephone to speak to someone.
vocabularies.example:
Call the doctor.
I'll call you later.
Call my name.
quizzes: I will __________ you on the telephone later today to tell you about my school project.
Please __________ the doctor if you don't feel better after you take your medicine now.
Did you __________ my name? I thought I heard someone talking to me in the garden.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling healthy again after being sick.
vocabularies.example:
I feel better today.
This is better than that.
I hope you get better soon.
quizzes: I hope you feel much __________ tomorrow so you can come back to school and play with us.
After I took some rest and drank some warm water, my headache felt a lot __________.
This new red pen is much __________ than my old one; it writes very smoothly now.

vocabularies.definition: Healthy and not sick.
vocabularies.example:
I'm very well, thank you.
He isn't well today.
Get well soon.
quizzes: I am feeling very __________, thank you! How are you doing today at your new school?
The little boy didn't feel very __________ this morning, so he stayed in bed to rest.
Drink lots of water and eat good food to stay __________ and strong all year long.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling sick or not healthy; unwell.
vocabularies.example:
He is ill and stays at home.
I feel ill.
Are you ill?
quizzes: He is staying at home today because he feels very __________ and has a high temperature.
If you feel __________ while you are in class, you should tell your teacher right away.
Are you feeling __________? Your face looks a little bit pale and you are very quiet today.

Sit in the bath.
quizzes: I love to have a long and hot __________ with lots of white bubbles after a very busy day.

The small duck toy is floating in the __________, and I like to play with it while I wash.

vocabularies.definition: An object that you stand under to wash yourself with water.
vocabularies.example:
Have a quick shower.
Turn on the shower.
I like showers.
quizzes: I always have a quick __________ in the morning to help me wake up and feel very energetic.
Please turn on the __________ for me; I need to wash off all this sand and salt from the beach.
I like to sing my favorite songs very loudly when I am standing under the warm __________.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of furniture that you sleep on.
vocabularies.example:
Go to bed.
Make your bed.
A big bed.
quizzes: It is almost nine o'clock, so it is time for you to go to __________ and get some sleep.
I always make my __________ every morning by pulling up the sheets and the warm blanket.
I have a big and comfortable __________ with a soft pillow where I like to dream at night.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land next to a house where plants are grown.
vocabularies.example:
Play in the garden.
A beautiful garden.
Flowers in the garden.
quizzes: I like to play football with my friends in the green __________ behind our house on weekends.
There are many beautiful and colorful flowers growing in the __________ in front of our house.
The birds are singing in the tall trees in our __________, and it sounds very peaceful today.

vocabularies.definition: The room where food is prepared and cooked.
vocabularies.example:
Cook in the kitchen.
A clean kitchen.
Mom is in the kitchen.
quizzes: My mother is in the __________ now, cooking some delicious vegetable soup for our family dinner.
We have a very clean and bright __________ where we prepare all our tasty meals every day.
Please help me carry these heavy bags of food into the __________ and put them on the table.

vocabularies.definition: The main room in a house where people sit and relax.
vocabularies.example:
Watch TV in the living room.
A big living room.
Sit in the living room.
quizzes: Our family likes to sit together in the __________ and watch funny movies on Friday nights.
There is a large and very comfortable sofa in our __________ where I like to sit and read books.
Please bring the bowl of popcorn into the __________ so we can start watching the movie now.

vocabularies.definition: A room where people sleep.
vocabularies.example:
Sleep in your bedroom.
My bedroom is blue.
Go to your bedroom.
quizzes: My __________ is painted a beautiful light blue color, and it has many posters of space on the walls.
I go to my __________ when I am tired or when I want to play quietly with my favorite toys.
Please go to your __________ and get your school bag; we need to leave for school right now.

vocabularies.definition: A room with a bath or shower and a toilet.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the bathroom.
Clean the bathroom.
Is anyone in the bathroom?
quizzes: I am going to the __________ to wash my face and brush my teeth before I go to sleep tonight.
Is anyone using the __________? I need to have a quick shower before we go out to the park.
The __________ is very clean and has a large mirror where I can see my reflection every day.

vocabularies.definition: The house or flat where you live.
vocabularies.example:
Stay at home.
Go home.
Home sweet home.
quizzes: I like to stay at __________ during the holidays and play games with my brother and sister.
It is starting to get dark outside now, so it is time for us to walk back __________ for dinner.
There is no place like __________, especially when you have a warm bed and some tasty food.

vocabularies.definition: A building that people live in.
vocabularies.example:
A big house.
My house is pink.
Build a house.
quizzes: We live in a large and beautiful __________ that has a red roof and a very big green garden.
My best friend lives in a pink __________ just two streets away from where I live in the town.
It took many months for the builders to finish building our new __________ in the quiet street.

vocabularies.definition: A set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building.
vocabularies.example:
I live in a flat.
A small flat.
His flat is on the third floor.
quizzes: I live in a small but very cozy __________ on the third floor of a very tall and modern building.
His __________ is in the city center, and it is very close to the cinema and the supermarket.
Moving to a new __________ can be very exciting because you get to meet many new neighbors.

vocabularies.definition: On a higher floor of a building.
vocabularies.example:
Go upstairs.
My room is upstairs.
Is he upstairs?
quizzes: My bedroom is __________, so I have to walk up the wooden stairs to get to it every night.
Is your brother __________? I thought I heard him playing with his loud drums in his room.
The bathroom is located __________, just next to my parents' room and my sister's bedroom.

grammars.description: Liệt kê các đồ vật trong nhà.
grammars.example:
There is a blanket on the bed.
There are many toys on the shelf.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people keep their money safely.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the bank.
The bank is next to the shop.
Where is the nearest bank?
quizzes: My father needs to go to the __________ this morning to get some money for our holiday.
The large __________ is located right next to the toy shop in the middle of the town.
Where is the nearest __________? I need to put some of my savings into my new account.

vocabularies.definition: A small restaurant where you can buy drinks and light meals.
vocabularies.example:
Let's meet at the café.
I like the cake in this café.
A small quiet café.
quizzes: Let's meet at the small __________ on the corner and have some hot chocolate and cookies.
I really like the sweet strawberry cake and the fresh juice they serve in this __________.
It is a small and quiet __________ where my mother likes to go and read her favorite book.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you go to watch movies.
vocabularies.example:
We saw a movie at the cinema.
The cinema is in the city center.
Let's go to the cinema.
quizzes: We went to the __________ with our whole class to watch a very funny movie about animals.
The new __________ is in the city center, and it has many large screens for watching movies.
Let's go to the __________ on Saturday afternoon and watch the new cartoon about a brave little dog.

grammars.description: Cách chỉ đường cơ bản.
grammars.example: Go straight on and turn left at the park.

vocabularies.definition: A sport played with rackets and a shuttlecock over a net.
vocabularies.example:
I play badminton with my sister.
Badminton is fun.
He has a badminton racket.
quizzes: I like to play __________ with my older sister in the garden using our new rackets.
__________ is a very fun sport where you hit a white shuttlecock over a high net.
He has a new __________ racket that is very light and helps him hit the shuttlecock very fast.

vocabularies.definition: A game played with a bat and a ball by two teams of nine players.
vocabularies.example:
He plays baseball at school.
A baseball bat.
Do you like baseball?
quizzes: He plays __________ at school every Tuesday afternoon with his friends on the large field.
The player used a heavy wooden __________ bat to hit the white ball as far as he could.
Do you like to play __________? Yes, I am the pitcher for our school's junior team.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two teams try to throw a ball through a net.
vocabularies.example:
He's very good at basketball.
A basketball hoop.
Play basketball in the park.
quizzes: He is very tall, so he is very good at playing __________ for our school's junior team.
I finally threw the heavy orange ball through the __________ hoop and scored two points!
We like to play __________ in the park near our house when the weather is warm and sunny.

vocabularies.definition: A game where two teams try to kick a ball into a goal.
vocabularies.example:
I love playing football.
A football match.
Kick the football.
quizzes: I love playing __________ with my friends after school, and I always try to kick the ball into the goal.
There is an exciting __________ match on TV tonight between two very famous and strong teams.
Can you kick the __________ very far? I want to see if you can reach the other side of the field.

vocabularies.definition: A game played on ice or grass with sticks and a puck or ball.
vocabularies.example:
They play hockey on grass.
A hockey stick.
Is hockey difficult?
quizzes: They play __________ on the green grass using long sticks and a small white ball during the summer.
A __________ stick is used to hit the ball or the puck into the goal to score points for the team.
Is __________ a difficult sport to learn? My brother says it takes a lot of practice to be good.

vocabularies.definition: A game played on a table with small rackets and a ball.
vocabularies.example:
We play table tennis in the garage.
A small table tennis ball.
I'm good at table tennis.
quizzes: We like to play __________ in the garage because we have a large green table and small rackets.
Be careful with that small white __________ ball; it is very light and can break very easily.
I am becoming very good at __________, and I can even hit the ball very fast now.

vocabularies.definition: A game played on a court with rackets and a ball.
vocabularies.example:
Play tennis on the court.
A tennis racket.
Do you want to play tennis?
quizzes: We went to play __________ on the new court near our school yesterday afternoon after class.
I have a new __________ racket that is very colorful and helps me hit the ball over the net.
Do you want to play __________ with me this Saturday? I have an extra racket you can borrow.

vocabularies.definition: To move on smooth ice or a flat surface using shoes with wheels.
vocabularies.example:
I like to skate in winter.
Can you skate?
Go to the skating rink.
quizzes: I like to go to the park and __________ on the flat path with my new rollerblades in the winter.
Can you __________? I am still trying to learn how to balance without falling down on the hard floor.
Let's go to the skating rink and __________ together; it is a very fun activity to do with friends.

vocabularies.definition: To move through water using your arms and legs.
vocabularies.example:
I swim every Friday.
Can you swim under water?
Swim in the pool.
quizzes: I go to the city pool to __________ every Friday afternoon to stay healthy and become stronger.
Can you __________ under water? I can hold my breath for a few seconds and see the bottom of the pool.
I like to __________ in the large pool at the sports center with my friends during the summer.

vocabularies.definition: To go up something using your hands and feet.
vocabularies.example:
Don't climb the tree!
Climb a mountain.
He likes climbing.
quizzes: Please don't __________ that tall tree in the garden! You might fall and get hurt very badly.
We want to __________ a small mountain during our summer holiday and see the beautiful view from the top.
He really likes __________ at the indoor center because there are many colorful walls to try.

vocabularies.definition: To move your body to music.
vocabularies.example:
She likes to dance.
A beautiful dance.
Let's dance!
quizzes: She likes to __________ to her favorite pop music in the living room when she is happy.
The little girl performed a beautiful __________ on the stage during the school's talent show.
Let's __________! Switch on the music and let's move our bodies together to the fun beat.

vocabularies.definition: To try to catch fish for sport or food.
vocabularies.example:
Go fishing at the lake.
I caught a big fish.
Fishing is relaxing.
quizzes: We like to go __________ at the calm lake during the weekend and see what we can catch.
I caught a very big __________ yesterday, but I put it back into the water so it could swim away.
__________ is a very relaxing hobby, especially when the weather is warm and the water is very still.

vocabularies.definition: To sit on and control an animal or a bicycle.
vocabularies.example:
Ride a bike.
Ride a horse.
Can you ride a motorbike?
quizzes: Can you __________ a bicycle? It is a very fun way to travel to the park with your friends.
I want to learn how to __________ a horse during our family's summer holiday in the countryside.
My brother is learning how to __________ a motorbike, but he must wear a helmet to stay safe.

vocabularies.definition: To move quickly on your legs.
vocabularies.example:
Run fast!
I run in the park.
A long run.
quizzes: You can __________ very fast! I think you could win a golden medal in the school's sports day.
I like to __________ in the park with my small dog every morning to stay healthy and energetic.
Let's have a long __________ together in the fresh air before we go back home for our breakfast.

vocabularies.definition: To make musical sounds with your voice.
vocabularies.example:
She sings very well.
Sing a song.
I like singing.
quizzes: She __________ very well, and she is going to perform a beautiful song at the school concert.
Let's all __________ a happy song together and clap our hands to the fun beat of the music.
I really like __________ in the school choir because all my best friends are there with me too.

vocabularies.definition: To move on your legs at a regular speed.
vocabularies.example:
Walk to school.
A long walk.
Walk in the rain.
quizzes: I always __________ to school with my mother every morning because it is very close to our house.
We had a very long __________ through the green forest yesterday and saw many colorful birds.
I like to __________ in the gentle rain and jump in the small puddles with my yellow boots.

vocabularies.definition: To use paint to make a picture.
vocabularies.example:
Paint a picture.
Paint the wall.
I like painting.
quizzes: I am going to __________ a beautiful picture of a colorful rainbow and a smiling sun today.
We need to __________ the living room wall a new color because the old paint is very dirty now.
I really like __________ in my art class because I can use many different and bright colors.

vocabularies.definition: To use a pencil or pen to make a picture.
vocabularies.example:
Draw a dog.
Draw with a pencil.
That's a nice drawing.
quizzes: Can you __________ a picture of a funny dog for me? I want to see how you use your new pencils.
I like to __________ with a sharp pencil because it helps me make very thin and neat lines.
That's a very nice __________, and you should hang it up on the wall for everyone to see today.

vocabularies.definition: To prepare food by heating it.
vocabularies.example:
I help my mom cook.
A great cook.
Cook dinner.
quizzes: I like to help my mom __________ our family dinner, especially when we make delicious pasta.
My father is a great __________, and he makes the best chocolate cake in the whole world!
Who is going to __________ dinner tonight? I think it is your turn to help in the kitchen.

vocabularies.definition: To look at and understand words in a book or magazine.
vocabularies.example:

Read a story.
I like reading comics.
quizzes: I like to __________ an interesting book before I go to sleep because it helps me have good dreams.

I really like __________ funny comics because they have many colorful pictures and funny jokes.

vocabularies.definition: To make letters or words on paper with a pen or pencil.

Write a letter.
Write in your notebook.

I am going to __________ a long letter to my grandmother and tell her all about my new school.
You should __________ the new words in your notebook so you can remember them for the quiz tomorrow.

vocabularies.definition: To give attention to a sound.

Listen to the teacher.
Are you listening?

Are you __________ to me? I am trying to tell you a very interesting story about my holiday.

vocabularies.definition: To look at something for a period of time.
vocabularies.example:
Watch TV.
Watch a movie.
Watch the birds.
quizzes: Let's __________ a movie together on Saturday afternoon and eat some crunchy popcorn in the cinema.
I like to __________ the colorful birds in the garden and see what they are eating from the tree.
We are going to __________ the big football match on TV tonight with our whole family.

vocabularies.definition: To do an activity for fun or as a game.
vocabularies.example:
Play games.
Play with toys.
Play a musical instrument.
quizzes: Let's __________ some fun games in the park after school today with our other friends.
I like to __________ with my new toys in my bedroom when the weather is rainy and cold outside.
Can you __________ a musical instrument? I am learning how to play the piano and the guitar.

vocabularies.definition: To push yourself into the air with your legs.
vocabularies.example:

Jump over the puddle.
Don't jump on the bed.
quizzes: Can you __________ high enough to touch the low branch of that old tree in the garden?
I like to __________ over the small puddles in the path when I wear my tall rubber boots.

grammars.description: Diễn tả sở thích.
grammars.example:
I like playing football.
She likes dancing.

vocabularies.definition: The first part of the day, before lunch.
vocabularies.example:

In the morning I have breakfast.
A sunny morning.

In the __________ I have my breakfast and then I get ready to go to my school.
It is a very beautiful and sunny __________, so let's go for a walk in the green park.

vocabularies.definition: The part of the day between lunch and the evening.

Good afternoon!
See you this afternoon.

Good afternoon! I am very happy to see you today; would you like to have some juice?
See you this __________ at the sports center! We can play some fun games together.

vocabularies.definition: The last part of the day, before you go to bed.
vocabularies.example:
In the evening I watch TV.
Good evening!
A quiet evening.
quizzes: In the __________ I like to watch TV or read an interesting book before I go to sleep.
Good __________! I hope you had a very good day at school and enjoyed your lessons.
It was a very quiet and peaceful __________ in our house because everyone was reading.

vocabularies.definition: The time when it is dark and most people sleep.
vocabularies.example:
Good night!
Sleep at night.
Stars shine at night.
quizzes: Good __________! It is time to turn off the light and go to sleep in your warm bed.
I always sleep at __________ so that I can have lots of energy for the next busy day.
The bright stars shine in the dark sky at __________, and they look very beautiful.

vocabularies.definition: On or during this day.

I am happy today.
What's the weather today?

I am feeling very happy __________ because my grandmother is coming to visit us at home.
What is the weather like __________? It looks like it might be a very sunny and warm day.

vocabularies.definition: On or during the day before today.

It was sunny yesterday.
Where were you yesterday?
quizzes: I went to the zoo with my family __________, and we saw many tall giraffes and loud monkeys.

Where were you __________ afternoon? I came to your house to play, but you weren't there.

vocabularies.definition: On or during the day after today.

See you tomorrow!
I will go swimming tomorrow.
Tomorrow is my birthday.
quizzes: See you __________! I hope you have a very good sleep and dream about magic dragons.
I will go swimming with my friends __________ afternoon at the large city swimming pool.
__________ is my eighth birthday, and I am going to have a very big party with my friends.

vocabularies.definition: Saturday and Sunday.

I play games at the weekend.
What are you doing this weekend?
quizzes: Have a nice __________! I hope you enjoy your time away from school and have lots of fun.
I like to play games and watch funny movies with my brother at the __________.
What are you doing this __________? I am going to visit my cousins who live in the city.

vocabularies.definition: The first part of the school or work week.

I go to school on Monday.
Monday is the first day of the week.
See you next Monday.
quizzes: I go to school on __________, which is the very first day of the school and work week.
__________ is the first day of the week, and I always have a lot of energy for my lessons.
See you next __________! Don't forget to bring your new book so we can read it together.

vocabularies.definition: The day of the week after Monday.
vocabularies.example:
We have English on Tuesday.
Tuesday morning.
Is it Tuesday today?
quizzes: We have our English and art lessons on __________ morning every week in our classroom.
