Is it __________ today? No, it is Monday, so we have to wait one more day for our art class.

vocabularies.definition: The day of the week after Tuesday.
vocabularies.example:
Happy Wednesday!
I swim on Wednesday.
Wednesday afternoon.
quizzes: Happy __________! We are in the middle of the school week now, so keep up the good work!
I usually go to the sports center and __________ on __________ afternoon with my sister.
On __________ afternoon, I like to go to the library and find some new books to read.

vocabularies.definition: The day of the week after Wednesday.
vocabularies.example:
I play football on Thursday.
Thursday evening.
See you on Thursday.
quizzes: I play football on __________ evening with my friends at the large field near the school.
On __________ evening, my father always cooks a very special and tasty dinner for us.
See you on __________! We can go to the cinema together and watch the new cartoon.

vocabularies.definition: The day of the week after Thursday.
vocabularies.example:
I love Friday.
School finishes on Friday.
Friday night.
quizzes: I love __________, because it is the last day of the school week and the weekend is coming.
School usually finishes early on __________, so I can spend more time playing in the park.
On __________ night, our family always stays at home and watches a funny movie together.

vocabularies.definition: The day of the week after Friday.
vocabularies.example:
I go to the park on Saturday.
Saturday is fun.
A busy Saturday.
quizzes: I like to go to the park with my dog on __________ when the weather is warm and sunny.
__________ is a very fun day because I don't have to go to school and I can play all day.
It was a very busy __________ for us because we went to the supermarket and the library.

vocabularies.definition: The day of the week after Saturday.
vocabularies.example:
I stay at home on Sunday.
Sunday is a day of rest.
Sunday morning.
quizzes: I usually stay at home on __________ and relax with my family before the new week starts.
__________ is traditionally a day of rest when many people spend time with their families.
On __________ morning, we often have a large breakfast with pancakes, eggs, and fresh juice.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling the time of day.
vocabularies.example:
It's seven o'clock.
I wake up at six o'clock.
See you at four o'clock.
quizzes: It is seven __________ now, so it is time for you to wash your face and have your breakfast.
I usually wake up at six __________ in the morning during the school week to get ready.
I will see you at the park at four __________ this afternoon! Don't forget to bring your ball.

vocabularies.definition: Thirty minutes after a particular hour.
vocabularies.example:
It's half past eight.
I have lunch at half past twelve.
Half past four.
quizzes: It is __________ eight, and the school bus will be here in exactly fifteen minutes from now.
I usually have my lunch at __________ twelve in the school canteen with all my classmates.
The movie starts at __________ four, so we have plenty of time to buy some crunchy popcorn.

vocabularies.definition: Fifteen minutes after a particular hour.
vocabularies.example:
It's quarter past nine.
School starts at quarter past eight.
Quarter past one.
quizzes: It is __________ nine now, and we should start our art lesson and draw some pictures.
Our school starts at __________ eight every morning, so please try not to be late today.
We are going to the library at __________ one this afternoon to find some interesting books.

vocabularies.definition: Fifteen minutes before a particular hour.
vocabularies.example:
It's quarter to ten.
I go home at quarter to four.
Quarter to seven.
quizzes: It is __________ ten now, and we have fifteen minutes before our break time starts.
I usually go home at __________ four after my school clubs and my football practice.
The train leaves at __________ seven in the morning, so we have to wake up very early.

vocabularies.definition: A word or symbol that stands for a quantity.
vocabularies.example:
What's your phone number?
A lucky number.
Pick a number.
quizzes: What is your father's phone __________? I need to call him and tell him you are staying late.
Seven is my lucky __________, and I always hope to see it when I play games with my friends.
Please pick a __________ between one and ten, and I will try to guess which one it is!

vocabularies.definition: The number 20.
vocabularies.example:
Twenty students in the class.
I am twenty years old.
Number twenty.
quizzes: There are __________ students in our class, and each student has their own wooden desk and chair.
My older brother is __________ years old, and he is studying science at the large university.
Please turn to page __________ in your English book and read the short story about the bat.

vocabularies.definition: The number 30.
vocabularies.example:
Thirty minutes.
It costs thirty dollars.
Number thirty.
quizzes: There are __________ minutes in half an hour, and there are sixty minutes in one full hour.
This new toy car costs __________ dollars, which is a lot of money from my small savings.
The number __________ comes after twenty-nine and before thirty-one on the number line.

vocabularies.definition: The number 40.
vocabularies.example:
Forty children.
Forty years ago.
Number forty.
quizzes: There are __________ children playing in the large park today because the weather is very nice.
My grandfather started working at the hospital __________ years ago when he was a young man.
Please find the number __________ on the board; it is the one between thirty-nine and forty-one.

vocabularies.definition: The number 50.
vocabularies.example:
Fifty percent.
Fifty cents.
Number fifty.
quizzes: More than __________ percent of the students in our class like playing football during the break.
I have __________ cents in my pocket, which is enough to buy a small piece of sweet fruit.
The number __________ is exactly half of one hundred, and it is a very important number to know.

vocabularies.definition: The number 100.
vocabularies.example:
One hundred dollars.
A hundred people.
One hundred percent.
quizzes: I have saved exactly one __________ dollars in my small blue bank to buy a new bicycle.
More than a __________ people came to watch the big football match at the city stadium yesterday.
I got one __________ percent on my math quiz today, and my teacher said she was very proud of me.

grammars.structure: Prepositions of time: On, At, In
grammars.description: Cách dùng giới từ chỉ thời gian.
grammars.example: On Monday. At six o'clock. In the morning.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with wings that flies in the sky.
vocabularies.example:
The plane is in the sky.
I travel by plane.
A big white plane.
quizzes: The white __________ is high in the blue sky, leaving a long white trail behind it today.
I really love to travel by __________ because it is very fast and I can see the clouds outside.
The big white __________ has many windows where passengers can look out at the beautiful world.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle that flies and has large blades that turn on top.
vocabularies.example:
A noisy helicopter.
The helicopter landed safely.
I saw a helicopter.
quizzes: A noisy __________ is flying over our house, and its large blades are turning very fast on top.
The orange __________ landed safely on the high mountain to help the people who were lost.
I saw a small __________ at the airport, and the pilot waved at me through the large window.

vocabularies.definition: A large vehicle that carries many people along a road.
vocabularies.example:
Go to school by bus.
Wait for the bus.
A red bus.
quizzes: I go to school by __________ every morning with all my friends who live in my quiet street.
We have to wait for the school __________ at the corner for exactly ten minutes every day.
The bright red __________ is very large and can carry many people to the center of the town.

vocabularies.definition: An extra-large bus used for long journeys.
vocabularies.example:
Travel by coach.
The coach is comfortable.
A long coach trip.
quizzes: We are going to travel by __________ to the seaside for our family's long summer holiday.
The __________ is very comfortable, and I can even sleep on the long journey to the city.
It was a very long __________ trip, but we saw many interesting things from the large windows.

vocabularies.definition: A large, strong vehicle used for carrying heavy things.
vocabularies.example:
A big truck.
The truck carries heavy boxes.
A blue truck.
quizzes: A big blue __________ is carrying many heavy wooden boxes to the large supermarket in the town.
The __________ carries many heavy things like stones and sand to the building site every day.
Look at that large blue __________! It has very big wheels and a powerful engine to pull heavy loads.

vocabularies.definition: A large truck used for carrying goods.
vocabularies.example:
The lorry is driving fast.
A delivery lorry.
Look at that big lorry!
quizzes: The large __________ is driving very fast down the motorway to deliver the fruits to the shop.
A delivery __________ parked in front of our house to bring my new bicycle that I ordered.
Look at that big __________! It is so long that it takes a very long time to drive past us.

vocabularies.definition: A strong vehicle used on farms for pulling heavy things.
vocabularies.example:
The farmer drives a tractor.
A red tractor on the farm.
I like tractors.
quizzes: The farmer drives a powerful green __________ to pull the heavy plow across the big field today.
There is a bright red __________ on the farm, and it is used to carry heavy bags of animal food.
I really like __________, especially when they have very large wheels and a loud engine sound.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle used for traveling on water.
vocabularies.example:
The boat is on the river.
A small fishing boat.
Sail a boat.
quizzes: The wooden __________ is on the calm river, and the children are using oars to move it slowly.
My grandfather has a small fishing __________ where he likes to spend his weekends on the lake.
Can you sail a __________? My father is teaching me how to control the sail and the rudder.

vocabularies.definition: A very large boat used for traveling across the sea.
vocabularies.example:
A big ship in the ocean.
The ship is sailing away.
A cruise ship.
quizzes: A very big __________ is in the deep blue ocean, carrying many people to a far away country.
The large __________ is sailing away from the harbor, and it looks very small on the horizon now.
We went on a very large cruise __________ for our holiday and stayed in a small cabin with a view.

vocabularies.definition: A long vehicle that travels on metal tracks.
vocabularies.example:
Travel by train.
The train is fast.
Wait for the train.
quizzes: We are going to travel by __________ to visit our grandmother who lives in a far away city.
The silver __________ is very fast, and it travels on long metal tracks through the green countryside.
We have to wait for the next __________ at the station; it will be here in exactly five minutes.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing with your legs.
vocabularies.example:
I ride my bike to school.
A new bike.
I love my bike.
quizzes: I really like to ride my __________ to school every morning because it is healthy and very fun.
My father bought me a new blue __________ for my birthday, and it has a shiny silver bell on it.
I love my __________, and I always wear my new helmet and my knee pads to stay safe when I ride.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with two wheels and an engine.
vocabularies.example:
My dad has a motorbike.
Motorbikes are noisy.
Wear a helmet on a motorbike.
quizzes: My dad has a powerful black __________ that he rides to his work in the city every single day.
__________ are very noisy vehicles, but they are also very fast and can travel easily in traffic.
You must always wear a heavy helmet on a __________ to protect your head and stay safe on the road.

vocabularies.definition: A person who drives a vehicle.
vocabularies.example:
The bus driver is kind.
A racing driver.
My dad is a good driver.
quizzes: The school bus __________ is very kind, and he always smiles and says hello to all the children.
A racing __________ has to be very brave and drive their fast car very carefully around the track.
My dad is a very good __________, and he always follows the rules of the road when he drives us.

vocabularies.definition: Someone who travels in a vehicle but is not the driver.
vocabularies.example:
The passengers are on the bus.
A busy passenger.
Wait for the passengers.
quizzes: The __________ are all sitting on the big bus, waiting for the driver to start the long journey.
It was a very busy bus with many __________, so I had to stand and hold on to the yellow bar.
Please wait for all the __________ to get off the train before you try to get on with your bag.

vocabularies.definition: To go from one place to another.
vocabularies.example:
I love to travel.
Travel by train.
Where do you want to travel?
quizzes: I really love to __________ to new and exciting places with my family during our summer holidays.
It is very fun to __________ by train because you can look out of the window at the beautiful view.
Where do you want to __________ during our next vacation? I want to go to the high mountains.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of paper that shows you have paid for a journey.
vocabularies.example:
Buy a ticket.
A train ticket.
Show your ticket.
quizzes: Please remember to buy a __________ from the machine before you get on the train to the city.
You must show your train __________ to the man when he comes to check it during our journey.
I have a __________ for the new movie at the cinema, and I am very excited to go and watch it.

vocabularies.definition: A journey to a place and back again.
vocabularies.example:
A school trip.
A long trip.
Enjoy your trip!
quizzes: We are all going on a school __________ to the interactive science museum next Wednesday morning.
It was a very long __________ to the seaside, but we had a lot of fun singing songs in the car.
Enjoy your __________ to London! I hope you see many interesting things and have a wonderful time.

vocabularies.definition: A period of time when you do not work or go to school.
vocabularies.example:
Go on vacation.
Summer vacation.
A fun vacation.
quizzes: We are going to go on __________ to the sunny beach next month and swim in the warm blue sea.
Summer __________ is my favorite time of the year because I can play all day and not go to school.
I had a very fun __________ with my cousins in the countryside, and we went to the farm every day.

vocabularies.definition: A container made of paper or cloth for carrying things.
vocabularies.example:
My school bag.
A heavy bag.
What's in your bag?
quizzes: I have my new school __________ ready with all my colorful books and my sharp pencils for tomorrow.
That is a very heavy __________! What do you have inside it? It looks very full of heavy things.
What's in your __________? I have my lunch box, a bottle of water, and my favorite small toy dog.

grammars.description: Nói về phương tiện di chuyển.
grammars.example:
They go to school by bus.
We travel by plane.

vocabularies.definition: A very high hill, often with rocks or snow on top.
vocabularies.example:
A high mountain.
Climb a mountain.
Snow on the mountain.
quizzes: The very high __________ has lots of white snow on top, and it looks like it is touching the blue sky.
My father wants to climb a __________ next summer, but he says it will be a very difficult and long trip.
There is always white snow on the high peak of the __________, even when it is warm in the city below.

vocabularies.definition: A high area of land that is smaller than a mountain.
vocabularies.example:
Walk up the hill.
The house is on the hill.
A green hill.
quizzes: We like to walk up the small __________ near our house and then run down very fast to the bottom.
The beautiful white house is on the __________, and from there you can see the whole green valley.
The __________ is completely covered in green grass and many small colorful flowers during the spring.

vocabularies.definition: A natural flow of water that goes across land into the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The river is long.
Swim in the river.
A boat on the river.
quizzes: The __________ is very long and deep, and it flows through the center of the city and out to the large sea.
We like to swim in the __________ during the hot summer days because the water is very cool and fresh.
I can see a small wooden __________ on the river, and the people inside are waving their hands at us.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of water that is surrounded by land.
vocabularies.example:
The lake is quiet.
A beautiful lake.
Fishing in the lake.
quizzes: The __________ is very quiet and peaceful today, and the water is as smooth as a large blue mirror.
It is a very beautiful __________, and many people come here to have a picnic and relax in the sun.
My grandfather likes to go __________ in the lake every weekend and see what he can catch for dinner.

vocabularies.definition: A large area of land covered with many trees.
vocabularies.example:
Trees in the forest.
Wild animals live in the forest.
A dark forest.
quizzes: There are many tall green trees in the __________, and it is very cool and dark inside even when it is sunny.
Many wild animals like bears and foxes live in the __________, so you should always stay on the path.
The __________ felt very dark and mysterious as the sun started to go down behind the tall wooden trunks.

vocabularies.definition: A thick forest in a hot, tropical part of the world.
vocabularies.example:
A thick jungle.
Monkeys live in the jungle.
Explore the jungle.
quizzes: The __________ is a very thick and hot forest where many exotic birds and plants grow very quickly.
Many loud monkeys and colorful parrots live in the __________, and they make lots of noise all day long.
The brave explorers went to explore the __________ to find the hidden temple that was lost for many years.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of land that is completely surrounded by water.
vocabularies.example:
Live on an island.
A small island.
Go to the island by boat.
quizzes: I want to live on a small __________ in the middle of the ocean where I can swim and play all day long.
It is a very small __________ with only a few palm trees and a beautiful white sandy beach all around it.
We are going to go to the __________ by a large boat because there are no bridges to get there from the land.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land on a farm used for growing crops or keeping animals.
vocabularies.example:
Cows in the field.
A green field.
Play in the field.
quizzes: There are many brown cows in the __________, and they are eating the fresh green grass and relaxing.
The __________ is very green and large, and it is a perfect place to fly a kite or play a game of tag.
Let's play and run in the __________ before it starts to rain and we have to go back inside our house.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land and buildings used for growing food or keeping animals.
vocabularies.example:
Work on a farm.
A noisy farm.
Live on a farm.
quizzes: My uncle and aunt work on a large vegetable __________, and they grow many sweet carrots and potatoes.
It is a very noisy __________ with lots of clucking chickens, oinking pigs, and many loud sheep too.
I would love to live on a __________ so I could help the farmer take care of all the baby animals.

vocabularies.definition: The colorful part of a plant that produces seeds.
vocabularies.example:
A beautiful flower.
Smell the flower.
A red flower.
quizzes: There is a very beautiful red __________ in the garden, and it smells very sweet in the morning sun.
My mother likes to smell the __________ in our colorful garden, especially after it has rained.
I am going to draw a bright yellow __________ with many petals and a green stem for my art class today.

vocabularies.definition: A common green plant that grows on the ground in gardens and fields.
vocabularies.example:
Sit on the grass.
Green grass.
Cut the grass.
quizzes: We like to sit on the soft green __________ in the park and have a picnic with our friends today.
The __________ is very green and fresh after the rain, and it feels very cool on our bare feet.
My father needs to cut the __________ in our back garden because it has grown very long this week.

vocabularies.definition: A tall plant with a trunk and branches made of wood.
vocabularies.example:
Climb a tree.
A tall tree.
Apples on the tree.
quizzes: I like to climb the old oak __________ in the garden and see if I can touch the lowest branch.
There is a very tall apple __________ in the middle of the field, and it is full of red fruits today.
The birds build their nests high up in the green __________, where they can stay safe from the cats.

vocabularies.definition: One of the flat green parts of a plant that grow from the stem or branch.
vocabularies.example:
A green leaf.
The leaf fell off the tree.
A small leaf.
quizzes: A single yellow __________ fell slowly off the tree and landed quietly on the wet ground today.
Look at this very small green __________! It has a funny shape and many very tiny thin veins.
This __________ is very green and shiny, and it grows on the plant that my mother keeps in the kitchen.

vocabularies.definition: More than one leaf.
vocabularies.example:
Autumn leaves.
The leaves are yellow.
Many leaves on the ground.
quizzes: In the autumn, the __________ on the trees turn beautiful colors like bright red, orange, and gold.
The __________ are yellow and brown now, and they are falling off the trees and covering the sidewalk.
There are many dry __________ on the ground in the park, and I like to hear them crunch under my boots.

vocabularies.definition: A living thing that grows in the earth and has a stem, leaves, and roots.
vocabularies.example:
Water the plants.
A small plant.
Green plants.
quizzes: Please remember to water the __________ in the living room every morning so they stay green and healthy.
My mother bought a very small green __________ from the market, and she is going to put it in a decorative pot.
These green __________ grow very quickly when it is sunny and warm, and they need lots of water and light.

vocabularies.definition: The surface of the earth that we stand on.
vocabularies.example:
Sit on the ground.
The ground is wet.
Hard ground.
quizzes: We like to sit on the __________ in the park and play with our toys when the weather is warm and dry.
The __________ is very wet and muddy after the heavy rain, so please try to stay on the stone path.
The __________ felt very hard and cold under my feet when I walked outside without my warm slippers.

vocabularies.definition: A large piece of stone.
vocabularies.example:
A big rock.
Sit on a rock.
Hard rock.
quizzes: There is a very big grey __________ in the garden, and I like to sit on top of it and read my book.
We found a very large and heavy __________ near the river, and it had many interesting Fossil imprints on it.
The __________ is very hard and cold, and it has lots of colorful lichen growing on its rough surface.

vocabularies.definition: A small piece of rock.
vocabularies.example:
Throw a stone.
A small stone.
Walking on stones.
quizzes: I like to throw a small __________ into the calm lake and see how many times it can skip across the water.
There are many small white __________ on the path in the forest, and they make a crunching sound when we walk.
We were walking on many small and smooth __________ on the beach, and they felt very funny under our feet.

vocabularies.definition: The space above the earth where the sun and stars are.
vocabularies.example:
The sky is blue.
Stars in the sky.
Look at the sky.
quizzes: The __________ is very blue today, and there is not even one small white cloud to be seen anywhere.
The bright stars shine in the dark __________ at night, and they look like many tiny points of light.
Look at the __________! I can see a big white plane with its silver wings flying high above the town.

vocabularies.definition: A bright point of light in the sky at night.
vocabularies.example:
Shining star.
A bright star.
Look at the stars.
quizzes: I saw a very bright and shining __________ in the sky last night, and I made a special wish on it.
A bright __________ is twinkling high above us, and it looks like a small diamond in the dark velvet sky.
Can you see all the __________ in the sky tonight? They are so many of them that I cannot count them all.

vocabularies.definition: The large round object that moves around the earth and shines at night.
vocabularies.example:
The moon is round.
Full moon.
Look at the moon.
quizzes: The __________ is very round and bright tonight, and it is lighting up the whole dark world below.
It is a beautiful full __________, and it looks like a large silver coin hanging in the dark night sky.
Look at the __________! It has changed its shape and now it looks like a thin white banana in the sky.

vocabularies.definition: The earth and all the people, animals, and plants on it.
vocabularies.example:
All around the world.
A big world.
Save the world.

vocabularies.definition: All the things in the world that are not made by people, such as animals and plants.
vocabularies.example:
Love nature.
Beautiful nature.
Protect nature.

vocabularies.definition: The air, water, and land in which people, animals, and plants live.
vocabularies.example:
Clean the environment.
Protect our environment.
Green environment.

vocabularies.definition: An area of sand or small stones next to the sea.
vocabularies.example:
I go to the beach in summer.
A beautiful beach.
Play on the beach.

vocabularies.definition: The large area of salty water that covers much of the earth's surface.
vocabularies.example:
Swim in the sea.
The sea is blue.
Sail on the sea.

vocabularies.definition: A substance that consists of very small grains of rock, found on beaches.
vocabularies.example:
Play in the sand.
Yellow sand.
Sand castles.

vocabularies.definition: The star that gives light and heat to the earth.
vocabularies.example:
The sun is hot.
Shine like the sun.
The sun is yellow.

vocabularies.definition: One of the very large areas of salty water that covers the earth.
vocabularies.example:
The big ocean.
Deep blue ocean.
Swim in the ocean.

vocabularies.definition: A very small town in the countryside.
vocabularies.example:
Live in a village.
A small quiet village.
My village is beautiful.

grammars.description: So sánh nhất (ngắn).
grammars.example:
The mountain is the highest.
This is the smallest island.

vocabularies.definition: Doing a lot of things or having a lot of work to do.
vocabularies.example:
The street is very busy.
I am busy with my homework.
A busy day.
quizzes: The main street in the city is very __________ today because many people are out shopping for toys.
I am very __________ with my English homework right now, so I cannot come outside to play football.
It was a very __________ day at school today because we had two interesting lessons and a sports match.

vocabularies.definition: Giving a lot of attention to what you are doing so that you do not have an accident.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful with the glass!
A careful driver.
She is a careful worker.
quizzes: Please be very __________ with that glass! It is thin and it might break if you drop it on the floor.
A __________ driver always looks at the road and follows all the traffic signs to stay safe today.
She is a very __________ worker, and she never makes any mistakes when she is writing her long stories.

vocabularies.definition: Able to learn and understand things quickly and easily.
vocabularies.example:
A clever student.
That's a clever idea.
He is very clever with computers.
quizzes: That young student is very __________, and she always knows the correct answer to all the teacher's questions.
That is a very __________ idea! I think it will help us solve the problem and win the fun game today.
My brother is very __________ with computers, and he can fix any problem that we have with ours.

vocabularies.definition: Not easy and needing a lot of effort to do.
vocabularies.example:
This is a difficult question.
The test was very difficult.
English is not difficult.
quizzes: This is a very __________ question, and I need to think for a long time before I can give you the answer.
The English test was very __________ today, but I tried my best to answer every single question correctly.
Learning how to speak English is not __________, especially if you practice every day with your friends.

vocabularies.definition: Not difficult and not needing much effort to do.
vocabularies.example:
This is an easy game.
The homework was easy.
A very easy task.
quizzes: This is a very __________ game, and even my baby sister can understand the rules and play with us today.
The math homework was very __________ last night, and I finished it in only ten minutes after school.
Finding the hidden treasure was a very __________ task because the map was very clear and easy to read.

vocabularies.definition: Making you feel very happy and enthusiastic.
vocabularies.example:
An exciting movie.
The game was exciting.
That's exciting news!
quizzes: We watched an __________ movie about brave explorers and magic dragons at the cinema last Saturday afternoon.
The football game was very __________ today because our team won in the very last minute of the match.
That is very __________ news! I am so happy to hear that you are going to visit us this weekend.

vocabularies.definition: Known by many people.
vocabularies.example:
A famous singer.
This city is famous for its bridge.
He wants to be famous.
quizzes: She is a very __________ singer, and many people all around the world know her beautiful songs and voice.
This city is very __________ for its large silver bridge that crosses the deep blue river in the center.
My brother wants to be a __________ football player and play for the national team one day in the future.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling fear or worry.
vocabularies.example:
I'm frightened of spiders.
The child looks frightened.
Don't be frightened.
quizzes: I am a little bit __________ of large black spiders, especially when I see them in my bedroom at night.
The small child looks very __________ because he cannot find his mother in the busy supermarket today.
Don't be __________! The big dog is very friendly and he only wants to play with you and his ball.

vocabularies.definition: Helpful and caring about other people.
vocabularies.example:
She is a kind person.
Thank you for your kind words.
What kind of fruit do you like?
quizzes: She is a very __________ person, and she always helps other people when they are feeling sad or lonely.
Thank you very much for your __________ words; they made me feel much better after my difficult day.
What __________ of sweet fruit do you like the best? I really like juice red apples and yellow bananas.

vocabularies.definition: Making a lot of noise.
vocabularies.example:
Loud music.
A loud noise.
Don't speak so loud.
quizzes: Please don't play that __________ music so early in the morning! I am trying to have a peaceful sleep.
There was a very __________ noise coming from the kitchen, and it sounded like a glass bowl had broken.
Don't speak so __________ in the library! You must be very quiet so other people can read their books.

vocabularies.definition: Behaving badly or not doing what you are told (usually of children).
vocabularies.example:
A naughty boy.
Don't be naughty!
The dog was very naughty.
quizzes: That __________ boy was jumping on his bed and he broke one of the wooden legs this morning.
Don't be __________! You must listen to your teacher and follow the rules of the classroom today.
The small dog was very __________ and he chewed up my father's new leather shoes in the living room.

vocabularies.definition: Making very little noise.
vocabularies.example:
Be quiet!
A quiet village.
It's very quiet here.
quizzes: Please be __________! The baby is sleeping in the next room, and we don't want to wake him up today.
We live in a very __________ village in the countryside where there are no busy streets or loud noises.
It's very __________ here in the library, which makes it a perfect place to study and read books.

vocabularies.definition: Having a lot of money or expensive things.
vocabularies.example:
A rich man.
He wants to get rich.
Rich food is heavy.
quizzes: He is a very __________ man, and he has a large house with a swimming pool and many expensive cars.
My brother wants to get __________ one day so he can buy a private plane and travel all around the world.
I don't like eating very __________ food like chocolate cake every day because it makes me feel very full.

vocabularies.definition: Not hard or firm and feeling pleasant when touched.
vocabularies.example:
A soft pillow.
Soft fur.
This bread is very soft.
quizzes: I have a very __________ pillow on my bed, and it feels as light and fluffy as a small white cloud today.
The kitten has very __________ and warm fur, and she loves it when I stroke her gently after her dinner.
This fresh bread is very __________, and it tastes delicious with some sweet strawberry jam and butter.

vocabularies.definition: Very bad or unpleasant.
vocabularies.example:
A terrible noise.
The weather was terrible.
I feel terrible.
quizzes: There was a __________ noise coming from the old engine of the car, and then it stopped working completely.
The weather was __________ yesterday with lots of thunder and lightning, so we had to stay inside all day.
I feel __________ today because I have a very bad headache and a sore throat; I think I need to rest.

vocabularies.definition: Needing to rest or sleep.
vocabularies.example:
I'm very tired.
A tired child.
You look tired.
quizzes: I'm very __________ after our long walk in the forest today, and I think I will go to bed very early tonight.
The __________ child was rubbing his eyes and yawning during the movie, so his mother carried him to bed.
You look very __________ today! Did you stay up late last night reading that interesting book about bat?

vocabularies.definition: Not strong; having little physical power.
vocabularies.example:
A weak person.
I feel weak.
A weak tea.
quizzes: The old man felt very __________ after his long illness, and he needed a wooden stick to help him walk today.
I feel __________ and tired because I didn't have any breakfast this morning before I went to school.
The tea that my mother made is very __________, and it doesn't have much flavor or a strong dark color.

vocabularies.definition: More bad than anything else.
vocabularies.example:
This is the worst day of my life.
The worst movie.
That was his worst mistake.
quizzes: This is the __________ movie I have ever seen! It was very boring and the story didn't make any sense.
Losing my favorite toy dog was the __________ thing that happened to me during our family's beach holiday.
The __________ part of the English test was the long story at the end because it had many difficult words.

vocabularies.definition: Better than anything else.
vocabularies.example:
My best friend.
The best player.
You are the best!

vocabularies.definition: Not interesting and making you feel tired.
vocabularies.example:
A boring book.
The lesson was boring.
I'm bored.

vocabularies.definition: Brave people do things that are dangerous without showing fear.
vocabularies.example:
A brave soldier.
Be brave!
A brave thing to do.

vocabularies.definition: Having nothing inside.
vocabularies.example:
The box is empty.
An empty street.
An empty glass.

vocabularies.definition: Containing as much or as many as possible.
vocabularies.example:
A full glass.
The bus is full.
I'm full.

grammars.description: So sánh hơn với tính từ dài.
grammars.example:
This book is more exciting than that one.
English is more difficult than math.

vocabularies.definition: To say something to someone as a question.
vocabularies.example:
Ask a question.
Ask for help.
Ask the teacher.
quizzes: Please __________ the teacher if you don't understand the rules of the new game today.
You can __________ for help if the math question is too difficult for you to solve alone.
I want to __________ the teacher a question about the interesting story we read this morning.

vocabularies.definition: To say or write something back to someone who has asked you a question.
vocabularies.example:
Answer the question.
Answer the phone.
Know the answer.
quizzes: Can you __________ the question? It is on page ten of your colorful English book today.
Please __________ the phone! It has been ringing for a very long time in the living room.
I know the correct __________ to that question because I read it in my science book last night.

vocabularies.definition: To take someone or something with you to a place.
vocabularies.example:
Bring your books.
I'll bring some food.
Bring me a glass of water.
quizzes: Please remember to __________ your school books and your lunch box with you tomorrow morning.
I'll __________ some delicious food to the picnic, like sweet red apples and some orange juice.
Can you __________ me a glass of cold water? I am very thirsty after our long walk in the sun.

vocabularies.definition: To get something by paying money for it.
vocabularies.example:
Buy a new car.
Buy some bread.
Where did you buy that?
quizzes: I want to __________ a new toy car with the pocket money that I saved in my small piggy bank.
My mother is going to __________ some fresh bread and some milk from the local shop today.
Where did you __________ that beautiful blue dress? It looks very pretty and fits you perfectly.

vocabularies.definition: To hold something and move it from one place to another.
vocabularies.example:
Carry a bag.
Can you carry this?
Carry the baby.
quizzes: Can you help me __________ this heavy bag? It is full of many heavy books and it is very difficult.
Please __________ the baby very carefully! He is sleeping and we don't want him to wake up.
I saw a strong man __________ a large wooden box on his shoulder in the busy city center today.

vocabularies.definition: To decide which thing you want from a group.
vocabularies.example:
Choose a color.
Which one do you choose?
Choose your favorite toy.
quizzes: You can __________ your favorite color for the drawing, like bright red, deep blue, or sunny yellow.
Which one do you __________? I like the one with the funny monkeys and the colorful parrots.
Please __________ your favorite toy to bring with you on our family's long trip to the seaside.

vocabularies.definition: To let something fall to the ground.
vocabularies.example:
Don't drop the glass!
I dropped my pen.
Drop it in the box.
quizzes: Please don't __________ the glass bowl! It is very thin and it will break into many tiny pieces.
I __________ my blue pen on the floor, and now I cannot find it anywhere under my wooden desk.
You can __________ the old paper in the recycling box and help us keep our school clean and tidy.

vocabularies.definition: To see or get something after looking for it.
vocabularies.example:
I can't find my keys.
Look and find.
Find the cat.
quizzes: I can't __________ my silver keys! I have looked everywhere, even under the soft sofa in the hall.
Look and __________ the hidden toy dog in the picture; he is hiding behind a tall green tree.
Did you __________ the cat? She was sleeping in the warm sun on top of the old wooden fence.

vocabularies.definition: To receive or buy something.
vocabularies.example:
Get a present.
Get home.
I got a good grade.
quizzes: I hope I __________ a nice present for my birthday, like a new bicycle or a cool video game.
What time do you usually __________ home from school? I usually arrive at four o'clock today.
I __________ a very good grade on my English test because I practiced my vocabulary every day.

vocabularies.definition: To let someone have something as a gift or to provide it.
vocabularies.example:
Give me the book.
Give a present.
Give it to him.
quizzes: Please __________ me the red book that is on the small table; I want to read the interesting story.
My mother is going to __________ a present to my little brother for his fifth birthday next Saturday.
You can __________ it to him when you see him at the park today; he will be very happy to have it back.

vocabularies.definition: To do something for someone to make it easier for them.
vocabularies.example:
Help your mom.
Can you help me?
Help me with my homework.
quizzes: Please __________ your mom to clean the large kitchen after our delicious dinner this evening.
Can you __________ me to find my school bag? I think I left it under the old wooden desk in the hall.
I need some __________ with my English homework because some of the words are very difficult to spell.

vocabularies.definition: To go to a place where no one can find you or to put something where no one can see it.
vocabularies.example:
Hide behind the tree.
Hide the ball.
Play hide and seek.
quizzes: I like to __________ behind the tall green tree in the garden when we play games with my friends today.
Please __________ the small ball in a secret place so no one can find it easily during our game.
Do you want to play __________ and seek in the park? I'll count to twenty while you go and find a place.

vocabularies.definition: To have something in your hand or arms.
vocabularies.example:
Hold my hand.
Hold the baby.
Hold on tight.
quizzes: Please __________ my hand very tightly when we cross the busy street to stay safe from the fast cars.
My aunt is going to __________ the baby in her arms, and he looks very small and very happy today.
Please __________ on tight to the yellow bar on the bus when it starts to move away from the stop.

vocabularies.definition: To want something to happen or be true.
vocabularies.example:
I hope it's sunny tomorrow.
I hope you feel better.
Hope for the best.
quizzes: I __________ it's sunny and warm tomorrow so we can go to the big park and fly our colorful kites.
I __________ you feel much better soon after your bad cold; please drink lots of water and rest well.
We always __________ for the best when we have an English test, and we practice our words every day.

vocabularies.definition: To have information about something in your mind.
vocabularies.example:
I know the answer.
Do you know him?
I don't know.
quizzes: I __________ the correct answer to the teacher's question because I studied very hard last night.
Do you __________ him? He is the new student in our class, and he is very kind and very clever too.
I don't __________ where my blue pencil case is; I think I might have dropped it on the way to school.

vocabularies.definition: To make sounds to show that you are happy or think something is funny.
vocabularies.example:
Laugh at a joke.
Don't laugh at me!
A loud laugh.
quizzes: We like to __________ at a funny joke that my father tells us every evening during our family dinner.
Please don't __________ at me! I am trying my best to sing the new song in our English class today.
The clown made a very __________ laugh that made all the children in the circus feel very happy.

vocabularies.definition: To get knowledge or a skill by studying.
vocabularies.example:
Learn English.
Learn to swim.
Learn a new song.
quizzes: I want to __________ English so I can talk to people all around the world and make many new friends.
My little sister is going to __________ to swim next summer, and she is very excited about going to the pool.
We are going to __________ a new song about the colorful world in our music lesson next Wednesday.

vocabularies.definition: To try to find someone or something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm looking for my dog.
Look for your keys.
Look for a present.
quizzes: I'm __________ my small toy dog; have you seen him anywhere in the busy living room today?
Please __________ your silver keys! I think you left them on the small table in the hall.
My sister is going to __________ a nice present for her best friend in the large toy shop today.

vocabularies.definition: To be similar in appearance to someone or something else.
vocabularies.example:
She looks like her mom.
It looks like rain.
What does it look like?
quizzes: She __________ her mom because they both have beautiful curly hair and bright blue eyes today.
Look at those dark grey clouds! It __________ rain, so we should go back inside our house now.
What does your favorite toy __________? It is a small green dragon with long wings and big eyes.

vocabularies.definition: To not be able to find something that you had.
vocabularies.example:
Lose your keys.
Lose the game.
I don't want to lose.
quizzes: Please be very careful not to __________ your new silver watch when you are playing football today.
We don't want to __________ the game, so we must practice our skills and work together as a team.
I don't want to __________ my favorite book, so I always put it back on the wooden shelf after I read it.

vocabularies.definition: To have a particular meaning.
vocabularies.example:
What does this word mean?
I mean it.
What do you mean?
quizzes: What does this difficult word __________? I need to use my new dictionary to find the definition.
I __________ it! I am very happy that you are going to come to my birthday party next Saturday.
What do you __________ by that? I don't understand the story, could you please explain it to me?

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is necessary or very important.
vocabularies.example:
You must listen.
Must I go now?
I must finish my work.
quizzes: You __________ listen very carefully to the teacher's instructions so you know what to do today.
__________ I go to bed now? It is only eight o'clock and I am not feeling very tired or sleepy yet.
I __________ finish my English homework before I can go outside and play with my friends in the park.

vocabularies.definition: To must have something because it is necessary.
vocabularies.example:
I need some water.
Do you need help?
I need a new pen.
quizzes: I __________ some cold water because I am very thirsty after our long walk in the hot sun today.
Do you __________ any help with those heavy boxes? They look very difficult for you to carry alone.
I __________ a new sharp pencil because my old one is very short and I cannot write my story with it.

vocabularies.definition: To lift someone or something using your hands.
vocabularies.example:
Pick up your toys.
Pick up the phone.
Pick up a leaf.
quizzes: Please __________ your colorful toys from the floor and put them back in the wooden box now.
Can you __________ the phone? It is on the small table in the corner of the busy living room.
Look at that beautiful red __________ on the ground! I'm going to pick it up and show it to my mom.

vocabularies.definition: To know the meaning of something.
vocabularies.example:
I don't understand.
Do you understand the question?
I understand now.
quizzes: I don't __________ this difficult question; could you please explain it to me one more time?
Do you __________ the rules of the new game? We must follow them to have fun and play correctly.
I __________ now! Thank you for helping me with my English homework; it's much easier for me now.

grammars.description: Thì quá khứ đơn với động từ có quy tắc.
grammars.example:
I help -> I helped.
We play -> We played.

vocabularies.definition: Every one of a group or the whole of something.
vocabularies.example:
All children.
All the time.
That's all.
quizzes: __________ the children in our class are very happy because we are going on a fun school trip today.
I can see __________ the colorful stars in the dark sky tonight; they are many points of light.
Is that __________ for your breakfast? You should eat some sweet fruit and drink some milk too.

vocabularies.definition: Some, or every one of a group (used in questions and negative sentences).
vocabularies.example:
Any questions?
Do you have any brothers?
Pick any color.
quizzes: Are there __________ questions about the new English lesson? Please ask me if you don't understand.
Do you have __________ brothers or sisters? I have one big brother and two small baby sisters today.
You can pick __________ color you like for your beautiful drawing; there are many colored pencils here.

vocabularies.definition: One more person or thing of the same type.
vocabularies.example:
Another cup of tea?
Another day.
Choose another one.
quizzes: Would you like __________ cup of sweet tea? I can bring it to you with some delicious biscuits.
We are going to have __________ sunny day tomorrow, which is perfect for playing in the big park.
If you don't like this toy car, you can choose __________ one from the wooden toy box in the corner.

vocabularies.definition: Used to talk about two people or things together.
vocabularies.example:
Both friends.
I like both.
Both hands.
quizzes: __________ my friends are coming to my house tomorrow to play music and draw some colorful pictures.
I really like __________ of these toy dragons; they are both very cool and have long green wings.
Please use __________ hands to carry that heavy glass bowl very carefully to the large kitchen today.

vocabularies.definition: Every one of a group of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
Each student.
Each day.
Five dollars each.
quizzes: __________ student in the class is going to get a new colorful English book to read during the lesson.
We go to the park __________ day after school to play with our friends and run on the green grass.
The apples are fifty cents __________, so I can buy two of them with my small silver coin today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to talk about all the people or things in a group.
vocabularies.example:
Every day.
Every child.
Every time.
quizzes: I brush my teeth __________ morning before I go to school, and also every evening before I go to bed.
__________ child in the room was laughing at the funny clown who was wearing a big red nose today.
__________ time I see a bright rainbow in the sky, I feel very happy and I want to draw it in my book.

vocabularies.definition: All the people in a group.
vocabularies.example:
Everyone is here.
Hello everyone!
Everyone knows that.
quizzes: __________ is here today, and we are ready to start our exciting English lesson about animals.
Hello __________! I hope you are all having a very wonderful and very sunny day today in the park.
__________ knows that the sun is very big and bright and it gives us lots of light and warm heat.

vocabularies.definition: All the things in a particular situation.
vocabularies.example:
Everything is okay.
Tell me everything.
Put everything in the box.
quizzes: __________ is okay now; I have found my silver keys and my colorful school bag in the living room.
Please tell me __________ about your family's wonderful holiday to the high mountains and the sea.
Please put __________ in the wooden toy box before you go to bed tonight, so the room is tidy.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is not known or named.
vocabularies.example:
Someone is knocking.
I saw someone.
Someone left this.
quizzes: __________ is knocking on the blue door; could you please go and see who it is at this time?
I saw __________ walking in the garden last night, and they were wearing a long dark coat and a hat.
__________ left this small toy dragon on the soft sofa; does it belong to any of you children today?

vocabularies.definition: A thing that is not known or named.
vocabularies.example:
I have something for you.
There's something in my eye.
Eat something.
quizzes: I have __________ special for you in my pocket; it is very small, very shiny, and very beautiful.
There's __________ in my eye! Could you please help me to see what it is? It feels very tickly today.
You should eat __________ for your lunch, like a fresh red apple or a cheese and salad sandwich.

vocabularies.definition: Not anything; no single thing.
vocabularies.example:
Nothing is left.
There's nothing in the box.
I have nothing to say.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask about the number of times something happens.
vocabularies.example:
How often do you swim?
How often do you eat fruit?
How often is the bus?
quizzes: __________ do you go to the pool to swim? I usually go there twice a week with my big brother.
__________ do you eat sweet fruit? I eat a juice red apple or a yellow banana every single day.
__________ is the school bus? It comes to the corner of our street every thirty minutes today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask about the amount or price of something.
vocabularies.example:
How much is this?
How much milk do you want?
How much does it cost?
quizzes: __________ is this cool toy dragon? It costs five dollars and I have enough money to buy it today.
__________ milk do you want in your glass? I only want a small amount because I am not very thirsty.
__________ does it cost to go to the cinema? It is ten dollars for an adult and five dollars for a child.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask about the number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
How many books?
How many brothers do you have?
How many apples?
quizzes: __________ books are there on the wooden shelf? I can see ten colorful books about fast silver planes.
__________ brothers and sisters do you have? I have one big brother and two small baby sisters today.
__________ red apples are in the basket? There are six of them, and they look very sweet and very juice.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask for information about something.
vocabularies.example:
What is your name?
What are you doing?
What's the matter?
quizzes: __________ is your name? My name is Lily, and I am very happy to meet you in the park today.
__________ are you doing with that colorful paper? I am making a large card for my mother's birthday.
__________'s the matter? You look a little bit sad; did you lose your favorite small toy dog today?

vocabularies.definition: In or to what place or position.
vocabularies.example:
Where are you?
Where is the bank?
Where do you live?
quizzes: __________ are you going now? I am going to the large toy shop to buy a present for my best friend.
__________ is the bank? It is in the city center, next to the busy supermarket and the quiet library.
__________ do you live? I live in a small quiet village in the countryside with many tall green trees.

vocabularies.definition: At what time.
vocabularies.example:
When is your birthday?
When do you go to sleep?
When is the match?
quizzes: __________ is your birthday? It is on the fifth of July, and I am going to have a very big party today.
__________ do you usually go to sleep? I usually go to bed at nine o'clock after I brush my teeth.
__________ is the football match? It starts at ten o'clock tomorrow morning in the big school field.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask about which person or people.
vocabularies.example:
Who is that?
Who are your friends?
Who did you see?
quizzes: __________ is that boy with the curly hair? He is my new friend, and his name is Charlie today.
__________ are your best friends at school? My best friends are Lily and Daisy; they are both very kind.
__________ did you see at the busy cinema last night? I saw my teacher and some of my school friends.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask which person something belongs to.
vocabularies.example:
Whose bag is this?
Whose dog is that?
Whose book is on the table?
quizzes: __________ bag is this? It's very colorful and it has a small toy dog hanging from the silver zipper.
__________ dog is that in the garden? It's my neighbor's dog, and he is very friendly and very loud.
__________ book is on the small wooden table? It belongs to my sister; she was reading it this morning.

vocabularies.definition: For what reason.
vocabularies.example:
Why are you late?
Why are you crying?
Why is it cold?
quizzes: __________ are you late for the English lesson? I'm sorry, I missed the school bus because I was slow.
__________ are you crying? Did you fall down on the hard ground and hurt your small knee today?
__________ is it so cold in the room? I think someone left the large window open in the living room.

vocabularies.definition: Used to ask about a choice between two or more things.
vocabularies.example:
Which one do you like?
Which way is the park?
Which color is best?
quizzes: __________ one do you like the best? I really like the bright red toy car with the fast silver wheels.
__________ way is the park? You have to turn left at the pharmacy and then walk past the quiet library.
__________ color is the best for the sky? I think deep blue or light blue are the most beautiful colors.

vocabularies.definition: Used when comparing two things or people.
vocabularies.example:
Better than that.
Bigger than me.
He is older than his sister.
quizzes: This English book is much better __________ that one because it has many colorful pictures and stories.
My brother is much bigger __________ me, but I am much faster when we run in the big school field.
He is older __________ his little sister, but they both like to play with the same small toy dragons.

vocabularies.definition: At that time (usually in the past).
vocabularies.example:
And then what?
Eat and then play.
Then he went home.
quizzes: And __________ what happened in the story? Did the brave explorers find the hidden silver treasure?
First you must eat your lunch, and __________ you can go outside and play in the green garden today.
He finished his homework, and __________ he went home to play with his new puppy in the back hall.

vocabularies.definition: Coming before all others in time or order.
vocabularies.example:
The first time.
Come first.
My first book.
quizzes: The __________ time I went to the large cinema, I was a little bit frightened of the loud noises.
I want to come __________ in the race tomorrow; I have been practicing my running every single day.
This is my __________ book about dangerous sharks, and I think the pictures are very interesting today.

vocabularies.definition: Coming after all others in time or order.
vocabularies.example:
The last day.
Last week.
Come last.
quizzes: Today is the __________ day of our summer vacation, and I am feeling a little bit sad to go back.
We went to the beach __________ week with my family, and we swam in the warm blue sea all day long.
I didn't want to come __________ in the spelling competition, so I studied my words very carefully tonight.

grammars.description: Các từ để hỏi.
grammars.example:
Who is your teacher?
Where is your house?

vocabularies.definition: The number 0.
vocabularies.example:
Ten, nine, eight... zero!
Zero degrees.
Below zero.
quizzes: Ten, nine, eight, seven, six, five, four, three, two, one, __________! The silver rocket is taking off!
It is very cold today; the temperature is __________ degrees, and there is lots of white snow on the ground.
There is __________ water in the glass; it is completely empty and I am still feeling very thirsty.

vocabularies.definition: Coming after the first thing.
vocabularies.example:
The second child.
Wait a second.
My second book.
quizzes: The __________ child in the line is my little brother; he is wearing a bright green hat and a blue coat.
Wait a __________! I need to tie my shoelaces before we go out to play in the big garden today.
This is my __________ book about dangerous animals, and I think the pictures of the lions are very cool.

vocabularies.definition: Coming after the second thing.
vocabularies.example:
The third floor.
The third time.
A third of the cake.
quizzes: We live on the __________ floor of this large building, and we can see all the city from our balcony.
This is the __________ time I have visited the quiet library this week to find some interesting books.
I would like a __________ of the chocolate cake, please; it looks very delicious and very sweet.

vocabularies.definition: Coming after the third thing.
vocabularies.example:
The fourth of July.
The fourth person.
Fourth place.
quizzes: Today is the __________ of July, and we are going to have a big party with lots of colorful flags.
The __________ person in the line is my father; he is carrying a heavy bag full of fresh groceries.
My brother came in __________ place in the school race, and he got a small silver medal for his hard work.

vocabularies.definition: Coming after the fourth thing.
vocabularies.example:
The fifth day.
My fifth birthday.
A fifth of the class.
quizzes: Today is the __________ day of our summer holiday, and we are having a wonderful time at the beach.
I am going to have my __________ birthday party next Saturday, and I'll have a cake with five candles.
A __________ of the class is going to the music lesson today, while the others are staying for math.

vocabularies.definition: The number 60.
vocabularies.example:
Sixty minutes in an hour.
Number sixty.
He is sixty years old.
quizzes: There are __________ minutes in one hour, and there are sixty seconds in one minute as well today.
My house is number __________, and it is the one with the bright red door and the tall green tree.
He is __________ years old, and he still likes to go for long walks in the countryside every evening.

vocabularies.definition: The number 70.
vocabularies.example:
Seventy people.
Number seventy.
Costs seventy dollars.
quizzes: There were __________ people at the school concert last night, and they all clapped very loudly today.
The bus number __________ is the one that goes to the busy city center and the large quiet library.
This new toy dragon costs __________ dollars, which is a lot of money for a small child like me.

vocabularies.definition: The number 80.
vocabularies.example:
Eighty children.
Number eighty.
Eighty percent.
quizzes: There are __________ children in our school, and we all play together in the big green field today.
My grandmother is number __________, and she lives in a very pretty house with lots of colorful flowers.
__________ percent of the class finished their English homework early, so they could go out and play.

vocabularies.definition: The number 90.
vocabularies.example:
Ninety miles.
Number ninety.
He is ninety.
quizzes: It is __________ miles from our city to the high mountains, so it takes a long time to travel there.
The bus number __________ is the one that goes to the large hospital and the busy train station today.
He is __________ years old, but he is still very strong and he can walk up the stairs very quickly.

vocabularies.definition: The number 1,000 or many thousands.
vocabularies.example:
Thousands of people.
Three thousand birds.
Many thousands.
quizzes: There were __________ of people at the football match yesterday, and they were all cheering loudly today.
I can see three __________ birds flying in the sky; they are migrations to a warmer place for the winter.
There are many __________ of stars in the galaxy, and they look like small sparks of light in the night.

vocabularies.definition: A large amount or number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
A lot of toys.
A lot of time.
Thanks a lot!
quizzes: I have a __________ of toys in my room, but my favorite one is the silver rocket that flies to the moon.
There is a __________ of time before the school bus arrives, so we can play one more game in the garden.
Thanks a __________ for helping me to find my silver keys! I am very happy that they are not lost now.

vocabularies.definition: A large number of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
How many?
Many children.
So many books.
quizzes: How __________ apples are in the basket? I can see six of them, and they look very juice and sweet today.
There are __________ children in our school, and we all play together in the big green field every day.
I have so __________ books about fast silver planes that I need a new larger wooden shelf to put them on.

vocabularies.definition: A large amount of something.
vocabularies.example:
Too much milk.
How much?
Not much.
quizzes: There is too __________ milk in my glass; could you please take some out so I don't spill it today?
How __________ is this cool toy dragon? I want to buy it with the silver coins in my small piggy bank.
I don't have __________ time to play today because I must finish my English homework before dinner time.

vocabularies.definition: An amount or number of something that is not known.
vocabularies.example:
Have some water.
Some friends.
Some more.
quizzes: Would you like to have __________ cold water? You look very thirsty after our long walk in the hot sun.
I have __________ friends who like to play with toy dragons too, and we play together every Wednesday.
I want __________ more of that delicious chocolate cake, please; it is the best cake I have ever tasted.

vocabularies.definition: Not any of a group of people or things.
vocabularies.example:
None of them.
There is none left.
None at all.
quizzes: __________ of them are my friends; they are all new students in our class and I don't know them yet.
There is __________ left; the bowl is completely empty and there are no more sweet red apples for us today.

Farmer's market.

A kind nurse.
School nurse.

A good teacher.

A busy worker.

vocabularies.definition: A person who flies an aircraft.

The pilot flies the plane.
A brave pilot.

vocabularies.definition: A person who sings, especially as a job.

A famous singer.

I love that singer.

vocabularies.definition: A person who performs in a movie or play.

A famous actor.
A movie actor.
He is a good actor.

The clown is funny.
A colorful clown.
Funny clown face.

A scary pirate.
A pirate ship.
Look at the pirate.

Look at the __________! He has a colorful parrot on his shoulder and a very large silver sword tonight.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to find information about a crime.

A clever detective.
A police detective.
He solves crimes.

vocabularies.definition: A person who helps someone else in their job.

Shop assistant.
A helpful assistant.
Ask the assistant.

vocabularies.definition: The activity of buying and selling goods and services.

A big business.
Business meeting.
He's in business.

vocabularies.definition: A room or building where people work at desks.

Working in an office.
A quiet office.
Where is your office?

vocabularies.definition: A building where you can buy things.

A busy shop.

A busy market.

Find a job.

vocabularies.definition: The activity that you do to earn money.

I work in an office.
A lot of work.
Go to work.

Help the teacher.

Save the earth.
Save your work.

vocabularies.definition: To get something by paying money for it.

Bright light.

Bright colors.

vocabularies.definition: Easy to see through; transparent.

Clear water.
A clear sky.
Is it clear?
quizzes: The water in the small mountain stream is very __________; you can see the small silver fish swimming in it today.

A small blue box.
