quizzes: I __________ a lot of cold water after I ran in the large green park because I was feeling very thirsty today.
He __________ orange juice with his breakfast this morning; it was very fresh and it tasted very sweet today.
We __________ warm milk before we went to sleep last night; it made us feel very warm and very safe today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'give'.
vocabularies.example:
He gave me a book.
I gave him a present.
She gave a smile.
quizzes: He __________ me a very interesting story book about space for my birthday; I like reading it every night today.
I __________ him a colorful present for his party; it was a new toy robot that can walk and talk very well.
She __________ a big happy smile to everyone in the classroom this morning; she is a very friendly girl today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'take'.
vocabularies.example:
He took a photo.
I took my bag.
It took ten minutes.
quizzes: He __________ a beautiful photo of the bright yellow stars and the moon with his new digital camera tonight.
I __________ my school bag from the floor and went to catch the bus to school early this morning today.
It __________ ten minutes to walk to the busy market from my house; it is not very far if you walk fast today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'make'.
vocabularies.example:
I made a cake.
He made a mistake.
Made in Vietnam.
quizzes: I __________ a delicious sweet chocolate cake for my mother's birthday party yesterday; she liked it very much.
He __________ a mistake in his school homework, so he had to use his eraser and write the answer again today.
This beautiful wooden toy dragon was __________ in Vietnam; it has many colorful scales and a very long tail.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'sleep'.
vocabularies.example:
I slept well.
He slept for ten hours.
We slept at home.
quizzes: I __________ very well last night in my soft warm bed because I was very tired after playing in the park today.
The little cat __________ for ten hours on the blue sofa in the living room; it looked very happy and peaceful.
We __________ at my grandmother's house last Saturday night; she read us an interesting story about a brave knight.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'buy'.
vocabularies.example:
I bought a new toy.
He bought some bread.
What did you buy?
quizzes: I __________ a new toy robot from the busy shop in the city center yesterday with my extra pocket money today.
He __________ some fresh bread and sweet milk for our breakfast this morning at the small shop near the station.
What __________ you buy at the market? I bought a colorful kite and a large 100-piece puzzle of the big city.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'bring'.
vocabularies.example:
He brought his books.
I brought some food.
She brought her dog.
quizzes: He __________ his favorite English books to school today to show them to the teacher and his best friends today.
I __________ some delicious food for our picnic in the large green park; I have apples, bananas, and some cake.
She __________ her small dog to the garden to play with us; it is a very friendly dog with very soft fur today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'find'.
vocabularies.example:
I found my keys.
He found the money.
We found the cat.
quizzes: I __________ my missing silver keys under the soft blue sofa in the living room this afternoon; I am very happy.
He __________ some money on the ground while he was walking to the busy train station with his mother today.
We __________ the little kitten hiding behind the tall green tree in the back garden; it was very frightened today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'know'.
vocabularies.example:
I knew the answer.
He knew him.
We knew the way.
quizzes: I __________ the answer to the difficult question in the English lesson today, so I put my hand up high tonight.
He __________ him from school; they used to play together in the large green park every Wednesday afternoon.
We __________ the way home even though it was getting dark because we have lived in this big city for a long time.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'read'.
vocabularies.example:
I read a book.
He read the news.
We read a story.
quizzes: I __________ a very interesting book about dangerous sharks and the deep blue sea before I went to sleep tonight.
He __________ the news on his new smart phone this morning while he was eating his breakfast of fresh fruit.
We __________ a long story about a brave monster and a small toy dragon in our English lesson at school today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'write'.
vocabularies.example:
I wrote a letter.
He wrote his name.
She wrote a book.
quizzes: I __________ a long letter to my grandmother yesterday to tell her about my new school and my new friends today.
He __________ his name on the first page of his new school notebook with his best blue pen this morning today.
She __________ a beautiful story about a princess who lived in a high mountain in her English lesson at school.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'sit'.
vocabularies.example:
I sat on the chair.
He sat near the window.
We sat down.
quizzes: I __________ on the soft blue sofa in the living room and watched a funny video on the large television tonight.
He __________ near the window in the classroom so he could look at the tall green trees and the birds today.
We __________ down on the grass in the large park to have our delicious picnic of fruit and sweet cakes today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'run'.
vocabularies.example:
I ran fast.
He ran home.
We ran in the park.
quizzes: I __________ very fast in the large green park this morning to catch the school bus today.
He __________ home after school because he wanted to play with his new toy robot and colorful kite.
We __________ in the park with our big brothers and our best friends last Wednesday afternoon today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'sing'.
vocabularies.example:
He sang a song.
She sang very well.
We sang together.
quizzes: He __________ a beautiful song about a brave knight and a scary monster at the school concert last night.
She __________ very well during the music lesson today; the teacher said her voice was very sweet tonight.
We __________ together in the living room while my big brother played the red guitar and laughed today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'swim'.
vocabularies.example:
I swam in the pool.
He swam in the sea.
We swam yesterday.
quizzes: I __________ in the large swimming pool at the sports center yesterday afternoon; it was very fun and cool.
He __________ in the deep blue sea with his father during our holiday last summer; he saw many small fish.
We __________ yesterday even though the water was a little bit cold; we used our colorful towels to get dry.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'lose'.
vocabularies.example:
I lost my pen.
He lost the game.
We lost the way.
quizzes: I __________ my best pink pen at school yesterday, and I am very sad because I can't find it anywhere today.
He __________ the game of hide and seek because he coughed loudly while he was hiding behind the tall tree.
We __________ the way to the big market this morning, so we had to ask a kind nurse for help to find it.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'win'.
vocabularies.example:
I won the race!
He won a medal.
We won the game.
quizzes: I __________ the race in the park this morning! I ran very fast and I got a small silver medal today.
He __________ a gold medal for being the best player in the football game last Saturday afternoon today.
We __________ the game of board games tonight; my team had the most points and we are very happy tonight.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'speak'.
vocabularies.example:
He spoke English.
She spoke quietly.
I spoke to him.
quizzes: He __________ English very well during the lesson today; the teacher was very happy with his hard work today.
She __________ quietly to her best friend lily in the classroom because they were in the middle of a lesson.
I __________ to my grandmother on the smart phone yesterday; she told me an interesting story about a cat.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'think'.
vocabularies.example:
I thought so.
He thought about it.
I thought you were at home.
quizzes: I __________ so too! I believed that the hidden treasure was under the soft blue sofa in the living room.
He __________ about it for a long time before he decided to buy the large 100-piece puzzle today.
I __________ you were at home! I went to your house this afternoon but nobody was there today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'understand'.
vocabularies.example:
I understood the lesson.
He understood me.
I understood everything.
quizzes: I __________ the lesson about space and the stars very well today because the teacher explained it clearly.
He __________ me when I told him about the missing silver treasure; he said he would help me to find it.
I __________ everything the pilot said about flying the large silver plane through the dark clouds today.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'hold'.
vocabularies.example:
I held the baby.
He held my hand.
She held the door.
quizzes: I __________ the little baby in my arms carefully; it was very small and it had very soft skin today.
He __________ my hand while we were crossing the busy road in the city center this afternoon today.
She __________ the door open for the old lady who was carrying a very heavy bag of fresh fruit today.

vocabularies.example:
He told a story.
I told the truth.
She told me a secret.
quizzes: My mother __________ me a very interesting story about a brave monster and a small toy dragon last night.
He __________ the truth about the missing silver treasure; he said he found it under the tall green tree.
Who __________ you about the party? Lily told me that it was going to be next Saturday afternoon.

grammars.description: Thì quá khứ đơn với động từ bất quy tắc.
grammars.example:
I go -> I went.
I see -> I saw.

vocabularies.definition: Used for getting someone's attention politely.
vocabularies.example:
Excuse me, where is the bank?
Excuse me, can you help me?
Excuse me, is this your bag?
quizzes: __________! Can you tell me where the busy train station is? I need to catch the fast silver train today.
__________! Can you help me to find my missing silver keys? I think I lost them in the large green park.
__________! Is this your colorful toy robot? I found it on the floor under the soft blue sofa today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for asking someone once more because you did not hear them.
vocabularies.example:
Pardon? Can you say that again?
I beg your pardon.
Pardon me.
quizzes: __________? I didn't hear what you said because the loud music from the radio was very noisy today.
I beg your __________! Could you please say that again more slowly and more clearly for me tonight?
__________ me! I didn't mean to bump into you while I was running to catch the school bus today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is not difficult or annoying.
vocabularies.example:
Thanks for help. - No problem!
No problem, I can do it.
It's no problem at all.
quizzes: 'Thanks for help with my English homework!' - '__________, I am happy to help you with your lessons anytime.'
__________, I can do it! I have a lot of free time this afternoon to clean the large wooden bookshelf today.
'I'm sorry I'm late for the party!' - 'It's __________ at all; we are just starting to eat the cake today.'

vocabularies.definition: Used to say 'yes' in a very sure way.
vocabularies.example:
Can I help? - Of course!
Of course you can.
Yes, of course.
quizzes: 'Can I help you to carry those heavy bags of fruit?' - '__________! That is very kind of you tonight.'
__________ you can play with my new toy robot! We are best friends and we can share all our toys today.
'Are you coming to my birthday party on Saturday?' - 'Yes, __________! I wouldn't miss it for anything today.'

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone not to worry about something.
vocabularies.example:
I'm sorry. - Never mind!
Never mind, it's okay.
Never mind about that.
quizzes: 'I'm sorry I lost your best pink pen!' - '__________! It's okay, I have another one in my school bag today.'
__________, it's okay! We can find something else to do if the large cinema is closed this evening tonight.
__________ about that! We can fix the small mistake in the story and it will be perfect for the lesson today.

vocabularies.definition: Used as a polite answer when someone thanks you.
vocabularies.example:
Thank you! - You're welcome!
You're very welcome.
You are welcome.
quizzes: 'Thank you for the beautiful colorful present!' - '__________! I hope you like the new toy robot today.'
'Thanks for the delicious sweet chocolate cake!' - '__________! I made it myself for your birthday today.'
'Thank you for helping me to find my keys!' - 'You are __________! I am happy to help my best friends anytime.'

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying goodbye in a friendly way.
vocabularies.example:
Goodbye, take care!
Take care of yourself.
See you, take care.
quizzes: 'Goodbye, __________! I'll see you at the school concert next Wednesday afternoon!' - 'You too, goodbye!'
__________ of yourself! Make sure you wear your warm red coat because it is very cold and grey outside today.
'See you tomorrow, __________!' - 'See you, I'll bring my new 100-piece puzzle for us to play tonight.'

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying goodbye when you expect to see them again.
vocabularies.example:
Goodbye, see you later!
See you later alligator.
Okay, see you later.
quizzes: 'Goodbye, __________! I'm going to the library to read a book about dangerous space monsters today.'
'__________ alligator!' - 'In a while, crocodile!' We like to say funny things when we say goodbye tonight.
'Okay, __________! Call me on your new smart phone when you get home to the big city tonight.'

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone success.
vocabularies.example:
Good luck with your test!
I wish you good luck.
Good luck to you.
quizzes: __________ with your English test today! I know you have worked very hard and you will do a very good job.
I wish you __________! I hope you win the gold medal in the race at the large green park this afternoon.
__________ to you! I hope you find the hidden silver treasure in the dark cave in the story game today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for praising someone who has done something well.
vocabularies.example:
Well done, you won!
A very well done task.
Well done everyone!
quizzes: __________, you won the race! You ran very fast and you are now the best runner in our school today.
A very __________ task! All your answers to the difficult questions in the lesson were completely right today.
__________, everyone! You all sang the song about the brave knight very beautifully at the concert tonight.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to hurry.
vocabularies.example:
Come on, we're late!
Come on, you can do it.
Oh, come on!
quizzes: __________, we're late for the school concert! We must run to the busy train station right now today.
__________, you can do it! Just climb the tall green tree and you will see the beautiful colorful birds.
Oh, __________! Don't be so grumpy; let's go to the large green park and play some funny games today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for warning someone that they are in danger.
vocabularies.example:
Look out! There's a car.
Look out for the dog.
Look out!
quizzes: __________! There's a fast silver car coming down the road! You must be very careful when you cross today.
__________ for the small dog in the garden! He likes to hide behind the tall trees and jump out at you.
__________! The floor is very wet and slippery because someone spilled their sweet milk this morning today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for warning someone of a danger.
vocabularies.example:
Watch out! It's hot.
Watch out for the cat.
Watch out!
quizzes: __________! It's very hot near the oven; you don't want to burn your hands while I am making the cake.
__________ for the little cat hiding under the soft blue sofa! She might jump out and frighten you today.
__________! There is a large grey elephant coming this way in the story game; we must hide in the cave.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to avoid danger.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful with that.
Please be careful.
Be careful!
quizzes: Please __________ with that expensive silver jewelry! It is very beautiful and we don't want to break it.
You must __________ when you walk in the dark forest at night; there might be some dangerous monsters tonight.
__________! Don't fall off the high mountain in your dream today; stay safe in your soft warm bed tonight.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone not to be upset.
vocabularies.example:
Don't worry, be happy.
Don't worry about me.
Everything is okay, don't worry.
quizzes: __________, be happy! Everything will be alright if we work together to find the missing silver treasure.
__________ about me! I am a very strong person and I can carry these heavy bags to the large house today.
Everything is okay, __________! We can simple clean the floor and write the story again in our notebook today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to act more quickly.
vocabularies.example:
Hurry up, the bus is here!
We must hurry up.
Please hurry up.
quizzes: __________, the fast silver bus is here! We don't want to miss it and be late for our school lesson today.
We must __________ if we want to get to the large cinema before the funny movie about monsters starts today.
Please __________! My father is waiting for us in the fast red car to take us to the busy market tonight.

vocabularies.definition: Used for telling someone to go more slowly.
vocabularies.example:
Slow down, you're driving too fast.
Please slow down.
Slow down a bit.
quizzes: __________, you're driving too fast! It's dangerous to go so quickly when there are many people in the street.
Please __________! I can't run as fast as you in the large green park because my school bag is very heavy.
__________ a bit! We have plenty of time to get to the sports center and we want to see the flowers today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a quick recovery from being sick.
vocabularies.example:
I hope you get well soon.
Here is a card, get well soon!
Get well soon, my friend.
quizzes: I hope you __________! I'll come to visit you in the hospital next Wednesday afternoon and bring some books.
Here is a colorful card I made for you, __________! I hope you feel much better after your bad cold today.
__________ my friend! We miss playing football with you in the large green park every Saturday afternoon today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for wishing someone a happy day on their birthday.
vocabularies.example:
Happy birthday to you!
Have a great happy birthday.
Happy birthday!
quizzes: __________ to you! I have a wonderful colorful present for you; it is a new toy robot that can talk.
Have a great __________ today! We are going to have a big party with sweet chocolate cake and many games.
__________, lily! You are six years old today and you look very beautiful in your new pink dress tonight.

vocabularies.definition: Used for making a suggestion.
vocabularies.example:
How about a pizza?
How about going to the park?
How about you?
quizzes: __________ a pizza for dinner tonight? We can go to the new Italian restaurant with our whole family today.
__________ going to the large green park to fly our colorful kites? The wind is just right for it today.
'I'm feeling very thirsty after the long race!' - '__________ some fresh cold water or some orange juice?'

vocabularies.definition: Used for asking someone what is wrong.
vocabularies.example:
What's the matter? Are you okay?
Tell me what's the matter.
What is the matter?
quizzes: __________? Are you okay? You look very sad and I want to help you to feel better today.
Tell me __________! I can help you to find your missing silver pen if you have lost it in the garden today.
__________? Why are you not playing hide and seek with Lily and the other children in the park today?

vocabularies.definition: Used for offering something to someone politely.
vocabularies.example:
Would you like some tea?
Would you like to play?
What would you like?
quizzes: __________ some sweet warm milk before you go to sleep in your soft bed tonight? It will make you feel safe today.
__________ to play with my new toy robot? It can move its silver arms and it has many bright red lights today.
What __________ for your snack? I have fresh red apples, sweet yellow bananas, and some chocolate cake today.

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you believe something is true.
vocabularies.example:
Is it sunny? - I think so.
I think so too.
Yes, I think so.
quizzes: 'Is it sunny outside today?' - '__________. I can see the bright yellow sun shining through the large windows tonight.'
'__________. I believe that the brave knight found the chest of gold coins hidden in the dark cave in the story today.'
'Yes, __________! It is a very good idea to go to the large cinema to see the funny movie about monsters tonight.'

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you want something to be true.
vocabularies.example:
Will you win? - I hope so!
I hope so too.
I really hope so.
quizzes: 'Will you win the gold medal in the race today?' - '__________! I have worked very hard and I want to be the best today.'
'__________. I want to see the beautiful colorful birds in the tall green trees in the park this afternoon today.'
'I really __________! I want everyone to have a lot of fun at my birthday party on Saturday evening today.'

vocabularies.definition: Used for saying that you do not have information about something.
vocabularies.example:
Where is he? - I don't know.
I'm sorry, I don't know.
I don't know the answer.
quizzes: 'Where is my big brother's red guitar?' - '__________. Maybe he left it in the quiet room on the second floor today.'
'I'm sorry, __________ where the library is! Maybe we should ask that helpful shop assistant near the station today.'
'__________ the answer to this difficult question! Can you please explain the English lesson to me again tonight?'

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is possible.
vocabularies.example:
Maybe it will rain.
Maybe next time.
Maybe yes, maybe no.
quizzes: __________ it will rain this afternoon, so we should bring our big umbrellas when we go out to the busy market today.
__________ next time you can come to my house and we can play with my 100-piece puzzle of the big city tonight.
__________ yes, __________ no! I am not sure if the fast silver train will be on time at the station today.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say that something is possible.
vocabularies.example:
Perhaps he is late.
Perhaps we can go.
Perhaps.
quizzes: __________ he is late because the bus was very slow today; let's wait for ten more minutes at the train station tonight.
__________ we can go to the zoo next holiday to see the large grey elephants and the monkeys in the tall trees today.
__________! I will ask my mother if I can stay at your house to play hide and seek after the school concert tonight.

vocabularies.definition: In addition; also.
vocabularies.example:
Me too.
It's too hot.
I like it too.
quizzes: 'I like to eat sweet chocolate cake!' - 'I like it __________! It is my favorite thing to eat for my birthday tonight.'
It's __________ hot to play football in the park today; let's sit under the tall green tree and read a book today.
I want to go to the cinema __________! Can I come with you and see the movie about the brave knights and monsters tonight?

vocabularies.definition: Used at the end of a negative sentence to mean 'also'.
vocabularies.example:
I don't like it either.
Me either.
Either one is fine.

vocabularies.definition: Used to agree with a positive statement.
vocabularies.example:
I like pizza. - So do I!
I'm hungry. - So am I!
So do I.

grammars.description: Các cụm từ giao tiếp thông dụng.
grammars.example:
You're welcome.
Never mind.

vocabularies.definition: A structure with walls and a roof, such as a house or school.
vocabularies.example:
A tall building.
An old building.
A modern building.

vocabularies.definition: An area with a wall or bars around it that is joined to the outside wall of a building.
vocabularies.example:
Stand on the balcony.
A flower on the balcony.
A small balcony.

vocabularies.definition: A machine that carries people up and down in a tall building.
vocabularies.example:
Take the elevator.
The elevator is broken.
A fast elevator.

vocabularies.definition: A door or gate that you go through to enter a place.
vocabularies.example:
Main entrance.
Wait at the entrance.
Where is the entrance?

vocabularies.definition: A door or gate that you go through to leave a place.
vocabularies.example:
Fire exit.
Emergency exit.
Find the exit.

vocabularies.definition: The surface that you walk on in a room.
vocabularies.example:
Ground floor.
First floor.
On the third floor.

vocabularies.definition: A part of a wall or fence that opens and closes like a door.
vocabularies.example:
Open the gate.
A wooden gate.
Wait at the gate.

vocabularies.definition: A room or passage just inside the main door of a building.
vocabularies.example:
Walking down the hall.
Dining hall.
A big hall.

vocabularies.definition: The surface that covers the top of a building.
vocabularies.example:
On the roof.
A red roof.
A leaky roof.

vocabularies.definition: One of the sides of a room or building.
vocabularies.example:
Paint the wall.
A brick wall.
A high wall.

vocabularies.definition: A structure built over a river or road so that people can cross it.
vocabularies.example:
Cross the bridge.
A stone bridge.
Under the bridge.

vocabularies.definition: A large strong building built in the past to protect people from attack.
vocabularies.example:
A big castle.
A king lives in a castle.
An old castle.

vocabularies.definition: A building where large amounts of goods are made by machines.
vocabularies.example:
Work in a factory.
A noisy factory.
A big factory.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you pay to stay and eat when you are traveling.
vocabularies.example:
Stay in a hotel.
A luxury hotel.
A cheap hotel.

vocabularies.definition: A large, beautiful house where a king or queen lives.
vocabularies.example:
A beautiful palace.
Royal palace.
Visit the palace.

vocabularies.definition: A large building with rows of seats around a field where people watch sports.
vocabularies.example:
Watch a match in the stadium.
A big stadium.
Football stadium.

vocabularies.definition: A tall narrow building or part of a building.
vocabularies.example:
A high tower.
The clock tower.
Climb the tower.

vocabularies.definition: The middle point or part of something.
vocabularies.example:
In the center.
City center.
Sports center.

vocabularies.definition: The point where two lines or surfaces meet.
vocabularies.example:
In the corner.
Around the corner.
Street corner.

vocabularies.definition: To go from one side of something to the other.
vocabularies.example:
Cross the road.
Cross the street.
Don't cross yet.

vocabularies.definition: To move your body or a vehicle to the left or right.
vocabularies.example:
Turn left.
Turn right.
My turn.

vocabularies.definition: A road or path that you use to go from one place to another.
vocabularies.example:
The right way.
Which way?
On the way home.

vocabularies.definition: A large and important town.
vocabularies.example:
Live in a city.
A big city.
A noisy city.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people live and work, larger than a village.
vocabularies.example:
A small town.
My hometown.
Go to town.

vocabularies.definition: A small town in the countryside.
vocabularies.example:
Live in a village.
A quiet village.
My village.

vocabularies.definition: A route for vehicles to travel on.
vocabularies.example:
Cross the road.
A long road.
Main road.

vocabularies.definition: A road in a city or town with buildings along one or both sides.
vocabularies.example:
Walk down the street.
A busy street.
High street.

grammars.description: Mô tả vị trí các tòa nhà.
grammars.example:
There is a hotel next to the bank.
There are many shops in the town.
